Johnson's 'Great Society' domestic programs, implying they have been sacrificed or corrupted by the escalation of the Vietnam War.

subject: Peace, Hope, Dove
description: A yellow button featuring a green illustration of a dove carrying an olive branch above the French text 'Je t'apporte une espérance', which translates to 'I bring you hope.'

title: I'M A CANADIAN YOUTH FOR PEACE
subject: Peace, Youth Activism, Anti-war
description: A circular button with a graphic of an orange sneaker with a peace symbol on its side crushing a pile of bayonets. The text 'I'M A CANADIAN YOUTH FOR PEACE' is written at the bottom.

title: AGAINST WAR すべての核をなくしましょう 地球を守る女たちの集会
subject: Anti-War, Nuclear Disarmament, Peace Movement
description: An anti-war and anti-nuclear button from Japan produced by the organization 'Women's Gathering to Protect the Earth'. It depicts a missile, a sun, and a flock of white doves.

title: NEJ TIL NEUTRONBOMBEN FREDSCENTRET GIRO 4 10 66 44
subject: Anti-nuclear, Peace movement, Denmark
description: A Danish anti-nuclear protest button from the Fredscentret (Peace Center) featuring a family silhouette inside a target, opposing the deployment of neutron bombs.

title: BRING the Troops Home Now
subject: Anti-war movement, Iraq War, Student activism
description: An orange circular button produced by the Campus Antiwar Network featuring the slogan 'BRING the Troops Home Now' in large white letters. It includes the organization's name at the top and their website address at the bottom.

title: DIABLO CANYON has it's FAULTS
subject: nuclear energy, anti-nuclear movement, environmentalism
description: Anti-nuclear power button regarding the Diablo Canyon Power Plant in California. The slogan refers to seismic faults discovered near the plant.

title: End Israeli Apartheid
subject: Israel, Palestine, Apartheid
description: A circular white button with the slogan 'End Israeli Apartheid' in black capital letters. In the center is a graphic of the Palestinian flag.

title: for peace pass SALT II
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Peace Movement, SALT II Treaty
description: A blue and white political button featuring a dove and text advocating for the passage of the SALT II treaty. The button was produced by the San Francisco (S.F.) Coalition for SALT II and bears a 'labor donated' mark.

title: NYC CLC AFL-CIO DINKINS '93 LABOR'S CHOICE FOR MAYOR
subject: Mayoral election, Labor unions, New York City
description: A blue campaign button from David Dinkins' 1993 New York City mayoral reelection campaign. It features a red apple in the center with the text 'DINKINS '93' and includes endorsements from the NYC Central Labor Council AFL-CIO.

title: Don't Waste Washington
subject: nuclear waste, environmentalism, activism
description: A button advocating against nuclear waste storage in Washington state, featuring green evergreen trees and a crossed-out radiation symbol. This slogan was associated with Initiative 383 in 1980.

title: GLOBALIZE the INTIFADA! www.njsolidarity.com
subject: Palestine, Intifada, Activism
description: A button featuring a graphic of a person in a keffiyeh using a sling against a mountainous background, with text reading 'GLOBALIZE the INTIFADA!' and the website for New Jersey Solidarity - Activists for the Liberation of Palestine.

title: NO NUKES IS GOOD NUKES
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Nuclear energy, Peace
description: A circular button with a dark green background and orange text that reads 'NO NUKES IS GOOD NUKES'. This slogan was used by activists protesting against nuclear power and nuclear weapons.

title: NO IRAQ WAR
subject: Anti-war, Iraq War, Peace
description: Rectangular white button with a red center containing blue text reading "NO IRAQ WAR". There are four stars on each side and one star in the letter "O" of "NO".

title: SOLIDARIDAD CON EL SALVADOR FMLN ¡EL SALVADOR VENCERA!
subject: El Salvador, Revolution, Solidarity
description: Red and white button featuring a group of individuals holding an FMLN flag, with the slogans 'SOLIDARIDAD CON EL SALVADOR' and '¡EL SALVADOR VENCERA!'.

title: HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR
subject: Anti-war, Foreign Policy, Central America
description: White button with red and blue text reading HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR and featuring a drawing of Uncle Sam. The logo for the International Socialist Organization (ISO) is at the bottom.

title: I'll vote Yes to keep NYC free of nuclear weapons.
subject: nuclear-free, disarmament, referendum
description: A black button with white text reading "I'll vote Yes to keep NYC free of nuclear weapons." It features a row of small red triangles along the top. This button was likely produced during the 1985 campaign to place a nuclear-free zone referendum on the New York City ballot.

title: MUTANTS for NUCLEAR POWER
subject: nuclear power, satire, anti-nuclear movement
description: A yellow and black satirical button from 1979 featuring a one-eyed smiley face and the text 'MUTANTS for NUCLEAR POWER', produced in Minneapolis, MN. It uses dark humor to protest the use of nuclear energy by referencing potential genetic mutations.

title: CONVERT ROCKY FLATS
subject: Anti-Nuclear, Disarmament, Peace
description: A white button featuring black stencil-style icons of bombs being transformed into a bus, with red text reading 'CONVERT ROCKY FLATS'. This button was part of a movement in the late 1970s and 1980s to shut down the Rocky Flats nuclear weapons plant in Colorado and repurpose it for peaceful, civilian manufacturing.

title: MLK DAY 2017
subject: Martin Luther King Jr., Civil Rights, Holiday
description: A commemorative pin for Martin Luther King Jr. Day 2017 featuring a stylized portrait of King against a red, white, and blue background.

title: Peace Action New York State: what are you doing to end war?
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Activism
description: A blue and white button from the organization Peace Action New York State. It features a graphic of a dove, the question "what are you doing to end war?", the slogan "Peace DEMANDS Action", and the website address www.panys.org.

title: FUND HUMAN NEEDS - RALLY JUNE 12 ABOLISH NUCLEAR WEAPONS
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Peace, Social Welfare
description: A button produced for the June 12, 1982, anti-nuclear rally in New York City's Central Park. It features slogans calling for the abolition of nuclear weapons and the funding of human needs, accompanied by illustrations of a peace dove, hands cradling a city and wheat, and a broken missile.

title: MARCH ON WASHINGTON JOBS PEACE FREEDOM AUGUST 27, 1983
subject: Civil Rights, Labor, Peace
description: A button commemorating the 20th anniversary March on Washington for Jobs, Peace, and Freedom held on August 27, 1983. The button features a portrait of Martin Luther King, Jr. and includes the logo of the United Food and Commercial Workers (UFCW) union.

title: IN MEMORIUM HIROSHIMA AUGUST 6, 1945 50th ANNIVERSARY
subject: Hiroshima, Atomic Bomb, Anniversary
description: Lapel pin commemorating the 50th anniversary of the atomic bombing of Hiroshima. The design features a white mushroom cloud silhouette inside a red circle, placed above a white rectangular base containing the commemorative text.

title: NO NUKES! ysa
subject: anti-nuclear, social movements, socialism
description: A button featuring the text 'NO NUKES!' and 'ysa' in green. YSA stands for the Young Socialist Alliance, which was the youth wing of the Socialist Workers Party and was active in anti-nuclear protests during the late 1970s and early 1980s.

title: AFRICAN-AMERICANS UNITED FOR DAVID DINKINS FOR ALL OF US DAVID DINKINS FOR MAYOR
subject: Mayoral Election, New York City, African-American History
description: A campaign button for David Dinkins' run for Mayor of New York City. Dinkins served as the 106th Mayor of New York City from 1990 to 1993, and was the first African American to hold the office. The button features the colors of the Pan-African flag.

title: BRING THE TROOPS HOME NOW! END THE OCCUPATION OF IRAQ
subject: Iraq War, Anti-war, ANSWER Coalition
description: A red rectangular pin-back button with black and white text calling for an end to the Iraq War. Produced by the A.N.S.W.E.R. Coalition (Act Now to Stop War and End Racism), it features the slogans "BRING THE TROOPS HOME NOW!" and "END THE OCCUPATION OF IRAQ" alongside the organization's website and name.

title: Give Peace A VOTE
subject: Peace, Voting, CODEPINK
description: A circular pink button with the slogan "Give Peace A VOTE" and the URL "codepinkalert.org". It features a black line drawing of a hand making a peace sign, with the index finger dipped in ink, a symbol of voting.

title: Join! the daily "hit-n-run" STRIKE! Stop this atrocity! @ 5PM Intermittent and ongoing!
subject: Iraq War, Anti-war, Protests
description: Anti-war button from the early 2000s, likely protesting the Iraq War, calling for daily 'hit-n-run' strikes at 5:00 PM to disrupt normal business as a form of protest.

title: GEEN NIEUWE KERNWAPENS IN EUROPA DEN HAAG, 29 OKTOBER
subject: Anti-nuclear protest, Nuclear weapons, Netherlands
description: A Dutch anti-nuclear protest button for the demonstration held in The Hague on October 29, 1983. The button features a cartoon character by Peter van Straaten and text translating to 'No new nuclear weapons in Europe'.

title: REFUSE THE CRUISE INTERNATIONAL DAY OF PROTEST 22 OCT. 83
subject: peace movement, cruise missiles, nuclear disarmament
description: A pinback button for an international day of protest against the deployment of cruise missiles in Europe on October 22, 1983, featuring a white dove and colorful plumes.

title: EXECUTE JUSTICE NOT PEOPLE
subject: Capital punishment, Human rights, Activism
description: A round yellow pin-back button with the hand-written text "EXECUTE JUSTICE NOT PEOPLE" in black. This is a common anti-death penalty slogan used in human rights campaigns.

title: Comprehensive TEST BAN NOW
subject: Nuclear testing, Disarmament, Peace movement
description: A yellow circular button featuring a black-outlined mushroom cloud with the words 'Comprehensive' in black and 'TEST BAN NOW' in red block lettering, advocating for a total ban on nuclear weapons testing.

title: THEY LIE
subject: Anti-nuclear power, Activism, Environment
description: Red button featuring the text 'THEY LIE' where the 'I' is replaced by a nuclear cooling tower. It is illustrated and signed by cartoonist Jules Feiffer.

title: IRAQ
subject: Iraq War, Peace, Activism
description: A circular black button featuring the word 'IRAQ' in large white letters. The 'Q' is stylized to incorporate a peace symbol within its circle. Small text at the bottom edge reads 'Copyright 2006 1stDawg.com'.

title: ATOMENERGIO? NE DANKON
subject: Nuclear power, Anti-nuclear movement, Esperanto
description: An Esperanto-language version of the 'Smiling Sun' anti-nuclear symbol, featuring the phrase 'ATOMENERGIO? NE DANKON', which translates to 'Nuclear energy? No thanks'.

title: radicalbuttons.com
subject: Politics, Activism, Website
description: The reverse side of a pin-back button featuring a silver metal edge, a black center, and a horizontal white sticker with the text 'radicalbuttons.com' printed in a blue sans-serif font.

title: NO MORE 9-11s US Out of the Middle East
subject: Anti-war, Terrorism, Foreign Policy
description: An anti-war button produced after the September 11 attacks, advocating for the withdrawal of United States forces from the Middle East as a means to prevent future terrorist attacks.

title: JOBS & JUSTICE SOLIDARITY DAY SEPT. 19, 1981 WASHINGTON, D.C. COALITION FOR ECONOMIC JUSTICE
subject: Labor, Economic justice, Protest
description: Yellow button with black text and illustration advertising Solidarity Day on September 19, 1981 in Washington, D.C., organized by the AFL-CIO and other groups to protest the policies of President Ronald Reagan.

title: no nukes is good nukes
subject: Anti-nuclear, Environment, Activism
description: A green and yellow pin-back button featuring the slogan "no nukes is good nukes," a popular phrase from the anti-nuclear movement in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

title: NUCLEAR FREE ZONE
subject: Anti-nuclear, Peace, Activism
description: A circular button with a grey background featuring a white mushroom cloud with a red diagonal slash over it, signifying opposition to nuclear weapons. Above the cloud is an arc of orange stick figures holding hands, and the text 'NUCLEAR FREE ZONE' appears in white at the bottom.

title: Long Live Palestine!
subject: Palestine, activism, solidarity
description: A button featuring colorful vertical stripes and an outline of the map of historic Palestine. It bears the slogan 'Long Live Palestine!' and the URL for New Jersey Solidarity, a pro-Palestinian activist group founded in 2002.

title: FOR OUR SURVIVAL STOP THE ARMS RACE SIGN THE STOCKHOLM PEACE APPEAL
subject: Cold War, Peace movement, Nuclear disarmament
description: A political button featuring a hand holding a quill, calling for the signing of the Stockholm Peace Appeal to stop the arms race during the Cold War.

title: SUPPORT PALESTINIAN LIBERATION STRUGGLE
subject: Palestinian liberation, activism, New Jersey Solidarity
description: This button features the text 'SUPPORT PALESTINIAN LIBERATION STRUGGLE' encircling a graphic of a person wearing a keffiyeh with a rifle slung over their shoulder. The bottom of the button displays the URL 'www.njsolidarity.com', which was the website for New Jersey Solidarity: Activists for the Liberation of Palestine, a group active in the early 2000s.

title: Washington Truth in Recruiting
subject: counter-recruitment, activism, military
description: The back of a button for Washington Truth in Recruiting (WATIR), featuring a sticker with the organization's name, website, and email address.

title: OPT OUT of the War Machine
subject: Pacifism, Anti-war movement, Vietnam War
description: A circular button featuring the slogan 'OPT OUT of the War Machine' surrounding a graphic illustration of two hands breaking a rifle, a symbol frequently associated with the War Resisters' International and the broader pacifist movement.

title: GUERRA NO! ICV
subject: anti-war, Iraq War, pacifism
description: A lime green button with white text that reads "GUERRA NO! ICV". This button was produced by the Catalan political party Iniciativa per Catalunya Verds as part of the mass protests in Spain against the Iraq War.

title: PREVENTION OF NUCLEAR WAR NURSES ALLIANCE
subject: disarmament, nurses, peace
description: A white button featuring a black and white mushroom cloud image behind a red prohibition symbol. Two grey cupped hands are positioned at the bottom, flanking the text "NURSES ALLIANCE". The organization was active in the 1980s advocating against nuclear proliferation.

title: End the sanctions now! Hands off Iraq!
subject: Iraq, Sanctions, Anti-war
description: A red and black protest button produced by the International Socialist Organization (ISO) advocating for the end of economic sanctions against Iraq and opposing military intervention.

title: RALLY FOR DISARMAMENT MAY 27 UN 1978 NYC
subject: Disarmament, Peace, Activism
description: A yellow button with red and green text promoting a disarmament rally in New York City on May 27, 1978, held near the United Nations headquarters during a special session on disarmament.

title: DPF
party: Democratic Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
subject: Palestine, Liberation, DPF
description: A white pin-back button featuring the black acronym 'DPF' in the center, surrounded by Arabic calligraphy that translates to 'Democratic Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine'.

title: Act Now to Stop War & End Racism no war no racial profiling
subject: Anti-war, Human Rights, Racial Profiling
description: Circular green button with purple and black text. 'Act Now to Stop War & End Racism' is written around the top edge. The center reads 'no war' and 'no racial profiling'. The bottom edge features the organization's website and phone number.

title: No War in Iraq
subject: Anti-War, Iraq War, Peace Activism
description: A circular orange button with black text that reads "No War in Iraq" followed by "DallasPeaceCenter.org" at the bottom, referencing a peace organization based in Dallas, Texas.

title: NWP Monogram
subject: Suffrage, Women's Rights, Feminism
description: A round yellow button featuring the black intertwined monogram of the National Woman's Party (NWP).

title: Women Against Nuclear Energy
subject: nuclear energy, feminism, environmentalism
description: A round blue button with the text 'WOMEN AGAINST NUCLEAR ENERGY' in yellow lettering. The center features a red and yellow Venus symbol with radiating yellow lines suggesting energy or radioactivity.

title: Greenpeace Stop Nuclear Testing Protest for No Test
subject: Greenpeace, nuclear testing, Nevada
description: A rectangular sticker or button on a wood background featuring the Greenpeace logo and a yellow outline of Nevada with the words "Stop Nuclear Testing". It includes a radiation symbol and a red star on the map, with the text "Protest for No Test" written diagonally.

title: NO WAR ON IRAQ
subject: Anti-war, Iraq War, Activism
description: A green button with white text expressing opposition to the Iraq War, featuring the website for the International ANSWER coalition.

title: Defend Palestine!
subject: Palestine, Activism, Handala
description: A yellow button featuring the character Handala, a famous symbol of Palestinian resistance created by cartoonist Naji al-Ali. The character is depicted from behind holding a sword shaped like a fountain pen nib. Below the text 'Defend Palestine!' is the URL for New Jersey Solidarity - Activists for the Liberation of Palestine.

title: The World Says No to War FEB 15 NYC
subject: Anti-war, Iraq War, Protests
description: A blue and black button featuring a globe with the text 'The World Says No to War FEB 15 NYC', referring to the February 15, 2003, global protest against the Iraq War.

title: Liberate Palestine
subject: Palestine, Liberation, Human Rights
description: A circular white button with black text reading 'Liberate Palestine' above and below an image of the Palestinian flag.

title: ABOLISH APARTHEID DIVEST NOW
subject: Apartheid, South Africa, Divestment
description: A circular political button featuring horizontal bands of red, black, and green, which are the Pan-African colors. A white map of Africa is centered on the button, with the southern tip of the continent highlighted in red. The text 'ABOLISH APARTHEID' is printed in black on the map, and 'DIVEST NOW' is printed in white along the bottom green section.

title: VOTE LIBERTARIAN
subject: Libertarianism, Elections, Political parties
description: The back of a political button from the Carol Moore collection. The front of the button features the text 'VOTE LIBERTARIAN' and a stylized white eagle logo on a blue background. The back shown in this image includes a horizontal pin and a white sticker for the website carolmoore.net/buttons.

title: U.S. OUT of the Middle East
subject: Gulf War, Anti-war, Middle East
description: A circular pin-back button with the message 'U.S. OUT of the Middle East' in red and blue text, accompanied by a cartoon illustration of Uncle Sam walking. The button likely dates to the protests surrounding the 1990-1991 Gulf War.

title: Close the Guantanamo concentration camps!
subject: Guantanamo Bay, detention camp, activism
description: A circular blue button with white text that reads 'Close the Guantanamo concentration camps!'. It is a political protest button against the US military prison at Guantanamo Bay.

title: PEOPLE FOR NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT ILLAWARRA
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace movement, Illawarra
description: A circular yellow button with green text and graphics for the Illawarra branch of People for Nuclear Disarmament. It features a broken missile and flowers growing along a central vertical line.

title: NO STAR WARS
subject: Strategic Defense Initiative, disarmament, anti-nuclear
description: This button expresses opposition to the Strategic Defense Initiative (SDI), a proposed missile defense system nicknamed "Star Wars" that was introduced by President Ronald Reagan in 1983.

title: Write in DICK GREGORY PRESIDENT FOR PEACE IN '68
subject: Peace, Election, Write-in
description: A political campaign button for Dick Gregory's 1968 write-in presidential campaign, emphasizing his platform for peace.

title: ATOMKRAFT? NEIN DANKE
subject: anti-nuclear movement, environment, energy
description: A yellow button featuring the Smiling Sun logo, which originated in Denmark in 1975 and became a worldwide symbol of the anti-nuclear movement. The text 'ATOMKRAFT? NEIN DANKE' is German for 'Nuclear power? No thanks'.

title: MOLESWORTH '85
subject: Peace, Anti-nuclear, Protest
description: Teal button with a white dove flying over wheat stalks, reading 'MOLESWORTH '85'. This button refers to the anti-nuclear peace camp and protests at the RAF Molesworth airbase in Cambridgeshire, England, during the mid-1980s.

title: NO COLONIAL OCCUPATION FROM IRAQ TO PALESTINE TO HAITI & EVERYWHERE!
subject: Anti-colonialism, Iraq War, Solidarity
description: A blue button promoting a protest on March 20, 2004, against colonial occupation. The text references Iraq, Palestine, and Haiti, and the button features the flags of these three nations. Below the text is an illustration of three interlocking fists symbolizing solidarity, and the website for NJ Solidarity is listed at the bottom.

title: BAN THE KRUGERRAND South Africa's Gold Coin VICTORY TO THE PEOPLES OF SOUTHERN AFRICA
subject: Apartheid, South Africa, Boycott
description: A yellow button with black text reading "BAN THE KRUGERRAND South Africa's Gold Coin" and "VICTORY TO THE PEOPLES OF SOUTHERN AFRICA". The center features an illustration of the continent of Africa with a clenched fist and broken chains positioned over South Africa, signifying resistance to apartheid.

title: SOLIDARITY with EL SALVADOR
subject: El Salvador Civil War, Activism, Solidarity Movement
description: A yellow pin-back button featuring a black silhouette of a person with a raised fist, surrounded by the text "SOLIDARITY with EL SALVADOR". This button likely dates to the period of the Salvadoran Civil War (1979-1992) and was used by activist groups in the United States to show support for popular movements in El Salvador.

title: JOBS. NO NUKES.
subject: Nuclear energy, Labor unions, Protests
description: A button for the March on Harrisburg on March 28, 1981, commemorating the second anniversary of the Three Mile Island accident. It features the text 'JOBS. NO NUKES.' in a central red circle, surrounded by text indicating the date, location, and the involvement of United Steelworkers Local 1010 in solidarity with coal miners.

title: PEACE - DREAMER BUT NOT THE ONLY ONE
subject: John Lennon, Peace, Imagine
description: A memorial button for John Lennon featuring his portrait, a peace sign, and lyrics from the song 'Imagine'.

title: Vote Disarmament Nov. 8 Yes
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Nuclear Freeze, Peace Movement
description: A campaign button advocating for a 'Yes' vote on a disarmament initiative or referendum. The date November 8 aligns with the 1983 election day, a year when many local nuclear freeze referendums were held across the United States. The button features a stylized red tower and a ballot marking symbol.

title: ©2007 RUTH KLEIN buttonbuddha @gmail.com 718-897-4746
subject: button making, contact information, copyright
description: The reverse side of a circular button, showing a safety pin fastener and a white rectangular sticker with purple text providing copyright and contact information for Ruth Klein's 'buttonbuddha' business. The same information is printed around the outer rim of the button.

title: I Support Pre-Emptive PEACE IN IRAN!
subject: Anti-war, Iran, Peace
description: A round black button with a red border and white text that reads 'I Support Pre-Emptive PEACE IN IRAN!'. The slogan is a play on the term 'pre-emptive war' or 'pre-emptive strike' used during the Bush administration's foreign policy debates regarding the Middle East.

title: FREE SOUTH AFRICA!
subject: Apartheid, Nelson Mandela, South Africa
description: A political button featuring a portrait of Nelson Mandela with a raised fist and the slogan 'FREE SOUTH AFRICA!'. The button uses the yellow, green, and black color scheme of the African National Congress (ANC).

title: El Mundo Dice No a la Guerra 15 FEB NY
subject: Iraq War, Anti-war, Protest
description: A blue button with a globe graphic and white Spanish text stating 'The World Says No to War' for the global day of anti-war protests held on February 15, 2003, specifically referencing the event in New York City.

title: Want peace? Get out! Palestine Afghanistan Iraq Now
subject: Anti-war, Middle East, Peace
description: A red and white political button calling for the withdrawal of troops and military intervention from Palestine, Afghanistan, and Iraq, featuring the flags of each country/territory.

title: No War in Iraq
subject: Iraq War, pacifism, protest
description: A yellow button with black text reading 'No War in Iraq' and the website for the Dallas Peace Center.

title: Grandmothers against THE WAR
subject: Protest, Peace, Anti-war
description: A black button with white and red text that reads "Grandmothers against THE WAR". This slogan is associated with the Grandmothers Against the War movement, which protested the Iraq War.

title: Support The Troops BRING THEM HOME NOW!
subject: anti-war, Iraq War, military withdrawal
description: A circular light blue button with bold black text. 'Support The Troops' is printed in an arch at the top, and 'BRING THEM HOME NOW!' is displayed in large, centered block letters. This slogan was a central message of the anti-war movement during the Iraq War, particularly promoted by organizations like Military Families Speak Out starting in 2003.

title: NUCLEAR WAR can spoil your whole day
subject: Nuclear war, disarmament, satire
description: A circular red button with black text featuring the satirical slogan 'NUCLEAR WAR can spoil your whole day.' This phrase was widely popular during the anti-nuclear proliferation movement and Cold War era of the 1980s.

title: NO CRUISE MISSILES PROTEST and SURVIVE
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Cruise missiles, Peace
description: An anti-nuclear protest button with the text 'NO CRUISE MISSILES' and 'PROTEST and SURVIVE'. It features a blue shield containing a castle, a dove, and an atomic symbol. The 'Protest and Survive' slogan was popularized by E.P. Thompson in 1980 as a response to a government civil defense pamphlet. The castle design indicates this button was likely produced for local protests in Exeter, England.

title: VICKI ROVERE War Resisters League
subject: Pacifism, Anti-war, Activism
description: The back of a name tag button for activist Vicki Rovere, showing a sticker with her email address vickirov@nycwhere.com. The front of the button identifies her as being part of the War Resisters League.

title: LOVE THE TROOPS HATE THE WAR
subject: Anti-war, Peace movement, Military
description: A pink circular button with black text that reads 'LOVE THE TROOPS HATE THE WAR', a slogan used to distinguish between support for soldiers and opposition to the conflicts in which they serve.

title: Drive Out the Bush Regime!
subject: Protest, Activism, Environmentalism
description: Square black button featuring a burning Earth with the text "Drive Out the Bush Regime!" in orange and the website "www.worldcantwait.org" in white. This is associated with the "World Can't Wait" movement founded in 2005 to protest the George W. Bush administration.

title: BLOCKADE DIABLO
subject: nuclear power, protest, activism
description: A red and white button featuring the words 'BLOCKADE DIABLO' and an image of two hands clasped in front of a nuclear power plant, used during protests against the Diablo Canyon Power Plant.

title: FREEZE & DISARM
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Peace Movement, Cold War
description: This button features a white dove, a symbol of peace, set against a blue background. The words "FREEZE" and "DISARM" are written in white around the edge, separated by an ampersand. It was likely produced during the nuclear freeze movement of the 1980s.

title: OREGON EUROMISSILE RALLY 22 OCTOBER 1983
subject: Euromissile Rally, Peace Movement, Nuclear Disarmament
description: Green and white button for the Oregon Euromissile Rally on October 22, 1983, featuring a peace dove with an olive branch over a map of Europe.

title: END THE OCCUPATION SUPPORT THE INTIFADA
subject: Palestine, Protest, Intifada
description: A circular button featuring the Palestinian flag (black, white, and green horizontal stripes with a red triangle on the left) with the text "END THE OCCUPATION" and "SUPPORT THE INTIFADA."

title: STOP NUCLEAR POWER
subject: anti-nuclear, protest, energy policy
description: A circular pin-back button featuring blue, hand-drawn style lettering on a white background that reads "STOP NUCLEAR POWER". This button reflects the grassroots anti-nuclear protest movements of the 1970s and 1980s.

title: URANIUM LEAVE IT IN THE GROUND
subject: uranium mining, environmentalism, anti-nuclear movement
description: Circular button featuring a brown mountain silhouette against a yellow background. At the top, "URANIUM" is printed in red arched text. Below the mountains, the phrase "LEAVE IT IN THE GROUND" is printed in white. This button was used by activists protesting against uranium mining, a prominent movement in the late 20th century.

title: NO NUKES NOW
subject: Nuclear energy, Activism, Environment
description: Round button featuring the phrase 'NO NUKES NOW' in large red capital letters on a dark background. Fine print along the upper edge indicates it was produced by CRC in Santa Cruz, California.

title: NO U.S. MISSILE DEFENCE CND
subject: Anti-nuclear, Peace, Missile Defence
description: A square blue button with the text 'NO U.S. MISSILE DEFENCE' in white stencil font, featuring a graphic of a missile and the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND) peace symbol and initials.

title: There are many roads to peace Nuclear weapons is not one
subject: Peace, Disarmament, Nuclear Protests
description: A blue pinback button featuring a white dove flying over several radiating paths or roads. The text 'There are many roads to peace Nuclear weapons is not one' is printed in white along the top edge.

title: FREE THE NEW YORK 102,624
subject: Prisons, Civil Rights, Black Panthers
description: This button calls for the release of the 102,624 individuals who were processed through the New York City prison system in 1970. It was likely produced in 1971 as part of a campaign by the Black Panther Party to protest mass incarceration and prison conditions in New York.

title: None
subject: Peace, Pacifism, Symbolism
description: Button featuring a black outline of a dove above two cupped hands, encircled by a laurel wreath, with a rainbow-colored chevron at the bottom.

title: Labor, Students Mobilize Our Power For Workers Strikes Against the War!
subject: Anti-War, Labor, Students
description: An anti-war button produced by the Internationalist Group featuring a purple fist and text calling for workers' strikes and student mobilization against the war, likely referring to the Iraq War.

title: WORKERS UNITE FOR MAY DAY PEACE, DISARMAMENT & FRIENDSHIP 85
subject: Labor movement, Peace, International Relations
description: A red button featuring white text and a white graphic of a dove-shaped hand holding a map of Australia. The button commemorates May Day 1985 and advocates for worker unity, peace, disarmament, and friendship.

title: END APARTHEID TERROR! SANCTIONS AGAINST SOUTH AFRICA!
subject: Apartheid, Sanctions, South Africa
description: A political button featuring the colors of the African National Congress (black, green, and yellow) with text calling for an end to apartheid and sanctions against South Africa, alongside the United Nations logo.

title: ROCKLAND COUNTY "I HAVE A DREAM" JAN. 15, 1971
subject: Civil Rights, Martin Luther King Jr., Commemoration
description: A white pinback button with black text that reads 'ROCKLAND COUNTY "I HAVE A DREAM" JAN. 15, 1971'. The button commemorates the birthday of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and refers to his famous 'I Have a Dream' speech.

title: SHUT DOWN
subject: nuclear energy, anti-nuclear movement, environmentalism
description: Blue button with red text reading 'SHUT DOWN' superimposed over a white line drawing of a nuclear power plant cooling tower. The button was produced by the Alabama Publishing Co. in Birmingham, Alabama, likely as part of the anti-nuclear power movement of the late 1970s and early 1980s.

title: I'm for PEACE
subject: Peace, Dove, Anti-war
description: A circular white button with green text that says "I'm for PEACE" and a green silhouette of a dove carrying an olive branch.

title: END ISRAELI OPPRESSION IN PALESTINE
subject: Israeli-Palestinian conflict, activism, human rights
description: A red pin-back button featuring white serif text that reads 'END ISRAELI OPPRESSION IN PALESTINE'. The button is an example of political protest material from the late 20th century advocating for Palestinian rights.

title: Demand Congress Bring the Troops Home OUT NOW! March on Washington Jan. 27 2007
subject: Iraq War, Anti-war, Protest
description: A political button calling for a march on Washington on January 27, 2007, to demand that Congress end the Iraq War and bring troops home. The center features the text "OUT NOW!" filled with photo imagery from the war, including the iconic image of a hooded prisoner at Abu Ghraib.

title: PEACE PLEDGE UNION Disarm for Life
subject: Pacifism, Disarmament, Peace
description: A blue button from the British pacifist organization Peace Pledge Union, featuring a tree and a white dove with the slogan 'Disarm for Life'.

title: MAKE JOBS NOT WAR DUMP BUSH!
subject: Iraq War, Labor, Election
description: A button produced by U.S. Labor Against the War (USLAW) featuring a dove perched on a fist holding a pencil and a wrench, symbolizing the intersection of peace and labor. It advocates for the removal of George W. Bush from office during the 2004 presidential campaign.

title: SUPPORT OUR TROOPS BRING THEM HOME NOW!
subject: Anti-war, Iraq War, Peace
description: A circular button with a white background featuring a black peace symbol and a yellow ribbon, signifying support for soldiers while advocating for their return from conflict. The text 'SUPPORT OUR TROOPS' and 'BRING THEM HOME NOW!' was a common slogan used by anti-war organizations during the Iraq War era.

title: I CHOOSE PEACE
subject: Peace, Activism, Anti-war
description: A circular button featuring a stylized white dove holding an olive branch in its beak against a blue background, overlaid with the text 'I CHOOSE PEACE' in teal lettering.

title: FOR A NUCLEAR FREE FUTURE THAT'S SANE
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Nuclear disarmament, SANE
description: A blue button with an orange sun and the text 'FOR A NUCLEAR FREE FUTURE THAT'S SANE', created by the Committee for a Sane Nuclear Policy.

title: Stop the War Machine MARCH 17
subject: Anti-war, Iraq War, Pentagon
description: This button was produced for the 'Stop the War Machine' march held on March 17, 2007, in Washington, D.C. The event marked the fourth anniversary of the beginning of the Iraq War and included a march to the Pentagon. The graphic depicts three missiles towering over the Pentagon building.

title: STOP THE WAR
subject: Vietnam War; Protest; Peace
description: A pinback button with a red peace symbol and the black text "STOP THE WAR" written across it.

title: NUCLEAR WASTE - DUMPING OUR FUTURE
subject: Nuclear Waste, Environmentalism, Activism
description: A red button featuring a graphic of the Earth inside a black trash can with the text 'NUCLEAR WASTE - DUMPING OUR FUTURE' around the outer edge.

title: DISARM!
subject: Disarmament, Anti-nuclear, Peace
description: A red and white button featuring a stylized dove silhouette and the word DISARM! at the bottom. It was produced by the Committee for a Sane Nuclear Policy (SANE) to advocate for nuclear disarmament.

title: Peace Action
subject: Peace, Activism, Anti-war
description: A button for Peace Action, the largest grassroots peace network in the United States, which was formed in 1993 by the merger of SANE and the Nuclear Weapons Freeze Campaign.

title: APARTHEID NO
subject: Apartheid, Human Rights, South Africa
description: A black and white political button featuring a stylized graphic of a person's face behind prison bars with the text "APARTHEID NO."

title: SOLAR EMPLOYS NUCLEAR DESTROYS
subject: Solar energy, Nuclear energy, Environment
description: A circular pinback button divided into yellow and green halves. The top yellow half contains the words 'SOLAR EMPLOYS' in red text, while the bottom green half contains the words 'NUCLEAR DESTROYS' in white text.

title: WELCOME PEACEFUL POLITICAL ACTIVISTS
subject: Protest, Activism, RNC
description: Light blue button featuring a stylized Statue of Liberty holding a sign that says 'PEACEFUL POLITICAL ACTIVISTS' with 'WELCOME' along the side. It was distributed during the 2004 Republican National Convention in New York City.

title: LONDON TO SIZEWELL MARCH FOR A SAFE FUTURE
subject: Anti-nuclear power, Protest, Environmentalism
description: A yellow protest button featuring green walking legs and a black silhouette of a nuclear power plant, produced for a 1984 march from London to Sizewell to protest against nuclear energy expansion in the United Kingdom.

title: SUN POWER NOW!
subject: solar energy, environmentalism, renewable energy
description: A light blue button featuring a yellow sun with red rays and the text 'SUN POWER NOW!' in red, advocating for solar energy.

title: STOP All Nuclear Explosions
subject: nuclear testing, anti-nuclear movement, disarmament
description: A circular pinback button featuring the text 'STOP All Nuclear Explosions' in white sans-serif font. The text is centered on a black circular background, which is partially overlapped on the left by an orange flame-like or sun-flare graphic set against a white border.

title: Troops home now!
subject: Anti-war movement, Iraq War, Peace
description: A red button with bold white text reading 'Troops home now!' calling for the withdrawal of military forces from international conflict, most commonly associated with protests against the Iraq War.

title: NO DRAFT NO WAR U.S. OUT OF EL SALVADOR
subject: Anti-war, El Salvador, Draft
description: A button protesting U.S. involvement in El Salvador and the potential reinstitution of the draft, featuring a green and black map of Central America and an illustration of a military helicopter.

title: I DON'T BUY SOUTH AFRICAN GOODS
subject: Apartheid, Boycott, South Africa
description: A white button with black text that reads 'I DON'T BUY SOUTH AFRICAN GOODS'. This button was part of the international campaign to boycott South African products in protest of the country's apartheid system of institutionalized racial segregation.

title: This button, 75+ item wholesale catalog & 100th Monkey $2 Islandia p.o. 1207 Felton CA 95018
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Anti-Nuclear Movement, Social Activism
description: The reverse side of a political button featuring a paper label from Islandia, a distribution center in Felton, California, associated with the anti-nuclear movement and the promotion of 'The Hundredth Monkey' book.

title: I CHOOSE PEACE
subject: peace, anti-war, dove
description: A circular button with a black background featuring a stylized light-colored dove holding an olive branch in its beak. The words "I CHOOSE PEACE" are printed in black capital letters over the dove's body.

title: No War in Iraq
subject: Iraq War, Anti-war movement, Peace
description: This button features the slogan 'No War in Iraq' in bold black letters against a bright lime green background. The website address 'DallasPeaceCenter.org' is printed at the bottom, indicating its origin from a peace advocacy group in Dallas, Texas.

title: Wij willen terug naar Palestina
subject: Palestine, Refugees, Human Rights
description: A Dutch language political button advocating for the Palestinian right of return. It features a stylized portrait of a Palestinian woman and an outline of a map of Mandatory Palestine.

title: NOT ONE MORE DIME NOT ONE MORE LIFE NONBINDING RESOLUTIONS ARE BUSHIT MARCH ON THE PENTAGON SATURDAY MARCH 17
subject: Anti-war, Iraq War, George W. Bush
description: Anti-Iraq War protest button from 2007, advertising a march on the Pentagon. The button features a graphic of a skull in a top hat with a dollar sign positioned over the Pentagon and uses a pun on President George W. Bush's name to express dissatisfaction with government policy.

title: globalise RESISTANCE
subject: Anti-globalization, Activism, Social movements
description: A circular white button featuring a red graphic of a raised fist, a symbol commonly used by activist groups to represent solidarity and resistance. Overlaid on the fist is the text 'globalise RESISTANCE' in black. This button represents Globalise Resistance, a UK-based anti-capitalist and anti-globalization organization that rose to prominence in the early 2000s.

title: USE ALTERNATIVE ENERGY
subject: Alternative energy, Solar power, Environmentalism
description: A yellow button with red text reading 'USE ALTERNATIVE ENERGY' around the perimeter. The center features a brown line drawing of a smiling sun, a palm tree, and an open hand reaching toward the sun.

title: THERE IS NO SHELTER FROM NUCLEAR WAR
subject: nuclear disarmament, anti-nuclear movement, civil defense
description: A circular button featuring a black background with a yellow radiation trefoil symbol. White text superimposed on the symbol reads "THERE IS NO SHELTER FROM NUCLEAR WAR".

title: Fighting For Justice
subject: Social justice, Human rights, Activism
description: A white button with the slogan "Fighting For Justice" and a black-and-white sketch of a small child viewed from behind. The imagery is characteristic of social justice and civil rights advocacy from the mid-20th century.

title: SUPPORTERS SOS OF SILKWOOD
subject: Labor activism, Nuclear safety, Whistleblower
description: A white button with black text that reads 'SUPPORTERS SOS OF SILKWOOD'. The 'O' in SOS is stylized as a Venus symbol (female symbol). This button was created by Supporters of Silkwood, a group advocating for justice and an investigation into the 1974 death of Karen Silkwood, a union activist and whistleblower at the Kerr-McGee nuclear plant.

title: IF WE CAN RISK NUCLEAR WAR WE CAN RISK DISARMAMENT
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace, dove
description: A circular pinback button featuring a gold-colored dove with an olive branch on a dark blue background. The text 'IF WE CAN RISK NUCLEAR WAR WE CAN RISK DISARMAMENT' is written in gold capital letters around the perimeter.

title: carolmoore.net
subject: Carol Moore, website, activism
description: The reverse side of a pin-back button featuring a white rectangular sticker with the text carolmoore.net in blue.

subject: Palestine, Peace, Solidarity
description: A political button featuring a hand making a peace sign in front of the colors and symbols of the Palestinian flag (green, black, and red).

title: NO NUKES
subject: anti-nuclear movement, environmentalism, peace
description: A button featuring the text 'NO NUKES' in large, dark blue or black letters. Below the text is a scene depicting a small house with trees on a white background.

title: U.S. OUT OF EL SALVADOR CISPES
subject: Anti-war, Foreign policy, El Salvador
description: Yellow button with black and red text and graphics protesting United States involvement in the El Salvador Civil War. The button includes the acronym CISPES, which stands for the Committee in Solidarity with the People of El Salvador.

title: INTERNATIONAL DISARMAMENT MOBILISATION FOR SURVIVAL
subject: Disarmament, Peace, Anti-nuclear
description: A blue circular button featuring a central graphic of a yellow hand gripping a broken silver sword, framed by a white laurel wreath. The text 'INTERNATIONAL DISARMAMENT MOBILISATION FOR SURVIVAL' is printed in black capital letters around the upper portion of the button. The use of the spelling 'Mobilisation' and the design suggests a likely origin from Australia or the United Kingdom in the late 1970s or early 1980s.

title: RESPECT AND PROTECT THE BLACK WOMAN
subject: Black Power, Women's Rights, Civil Rights
description: A circular white button with red text along the upper edge reading "RESPECT AND PROTECT". In the center, black text reads "THE BLACK WOMAN". This button reflects a slogan popularized by the Black Panther Party and the Black Power movement of the late 1960s and 1970s to emphasize the dignity and safety of Black women.

title: SOLAR ENERGY
subject: Solar power, Environment, Sustainability
description: A yellow circular button with a red stylized sunburst graphic. The words 'SOLAR ENERGY' are printed in red inside the sunburst, advocating for renewable energy.

title: TO REMEMBER IS TO END ALL WAR.
subject: peace movement, remembrance, pacifism
description: A square black pinback button featuring the gold text "TO REMEMBER IS TO END ALL WAR." at the top. Below the text is a gold illustration of doves carrying red poppies in their beaks.

title: ENOUGH! END THE ISRAELI OCCUPATION JUSTICE FOR THE PALESTINIANS
subject: Israel-Palestine conflict, Occupation, Justice
description: A black pinback button with white and red text. The center features the word 'ENOUGH' in large white letters followed by a red exclamation point-like block. Below this is the website address 'www.enoughoccupation.org'. Encircling the central design are the phrases 'END THE ISRAELI OCCUPATION' and 'JUSTICE FOR THE PALESTINIANS' separated by red dots.

title: JOBS NOT BOMBS U.N. RALLY JUNE 12
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace, labor
description: A round white button featuring a blue stylized bomb or missile with a white dove and olive branch integrated into its silhouette. The red text around the top-left edge reads 'JOBS NOT BOMBS', and the text on the right side reads 'U.N. RALLY JUNE 12', referring to the historic anti-nuclear protest in New York City in 1982.

title: LET'S STOP RACISM
subject: Civil Rights, Social Justice, Equality
description: Pink octagonal button with white text that reads 'LET'S STOP RACISM'.

title: Anti Nuclear Campaign
subject: Nuclear energy, Activism, Environment
description: A yellow badge with black text 'Anti Nuclear Campaign' and a graphic combining a red exclamation mark with two black wedge shapes to form a stylized radiation warning symbol. This button was likely produced for the Australian Anti-Nuclear Campaign in the late 1970s or early 1980s.

title: Fight Racism & Police Brutality
subject: Racism, Police Brutality, Civil Rights
description: This orange pin-back button features the words "Fight Racism & Police Brutality" in white bubble letters with a thick black shadow. The name of the sponsoring organization, "NATIONAL PEOPLE'S CAMPAIGN", is printed along the bottom edge in black.

title: FIGHT RACISM! RALLY May 1, 1976 FREE APARTHEID PRISONERS
subject: Anti-apartheid, Racism, Political prisoners
description: A circular yellow button featuring black text and graphics. At the top, it reads "FIGHT RACISM!" and at the bottom, "FREE APARTHEID PRISONERS". To the left is the text "RALLY May 1, 1976". The center graphic shows an outline of the continent of Africa containing a halftone image of a person's face behind vertical bars, symbolizing a prisoner.

title: Someone paid the price for your right to VOTE Selma to Montgomery March 1965 50th Anniversary 2015
subject: Voting Rights, Selma March, Civil Rights
description: A square button commemorating the 50th anniversary of the Selma to Montgomery March in 1965. It features a historic photograph of the marchers and the text "Someone paid the price for your right to VOTE".

title: Aldrig mere Krig
subject: pacifism, anti-war, peace
description: A white button with red text reading 'Aldrig mere Krig' (Never More War) and a blue illustration of two hands breaking a rifle, which is the logo of the Danish pacifist organization Aldrig Mere Krig, the Danish branch of War Resisters' International.

title: NO NUKES
subject: anti-nuclear, peace, activism
description: Blue circular button with a white peace dove and the text "NO NUKES" at the bottom.

title: WELL-MEANING GUARDIAN READERS AGAINST THE BOMB
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, The Guardian, Peace symbol
description: A circular button featuring a peace symbol (CND symbol) and the text 'WELL-MEANING GUARDIAN READERS AGAINST THE BOMB', referencing readers of the British newspaper The Guardian who supported nuclear disarmament.

title: NO NEUTRONBOMB
subject: nuclear weapons, anti-war, neutron bomb
description: Circular metal pin with 'NO NEUTRONBOMB' text. Two raised fists are depicted against a silver background with a red and black circular motif. The button was part of the anti-nuclear movement in the late 1970s and early 1980s protesting the development of enhanced radiation weapons.

title: End The Occupation
subject: Palestine, human rights, activism
description: A white button featuring the Palestinian flag in the center with the black text "End The Occupation" printed above and below it, advocating for the end of the Israeli occupation of Palestinian territories.

title: BUILD HOUSING NOT HIGHWAYS
subject: housing, infrastructure, activism
description: A white pinback button with blue text reading 'BUILD HOUSING NOT HIGHWAYS.' This slogan was commonly used during the 'highway revolts' of the 1960s and 1970s, where urban communities organized against the construction of large expressways that threatened to demolish residential neighborhoods and displace low-income residents.

title: Israeli war crimes are still war crimes
subject: Israel, War Crimes, Human Rights
description: A button featuring the text "Israeli war crimes are still war crimes" over a background of red and black stripes. The website "www.GlobalAware.org" is printed at the bottom edge.

title: FREE NELSON MANDELA
subject: Nelson Mandela, Anti-Apartheid, Human Rights
description: A yellow button with a green inscription reading 'FREE NELSON MANDELA' and a black and white stippled portrait of Mandela. It was produced as part of the international campaign to release Nelson Mandela from prison and end apartheid in South Africa.

title: WOMEN AGAINST APARTHEID
subject: Apartheid, Women, Activism
description: A circular black and white button featuring the text 'WOMEN AGAINST APARTHEID' and a logo that combines a female symbol with a yin-yang design.

title: INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY for DISARMAMENT MAY 24TH
subject: Women's rights, Disarmament, Peace
description: A round button promoting International Women's Day for Disarmament on May 24th. It features a hand-drawn white dove carrying a female symbol against a horizontally split purple and green background.

title: ©2006 RUTH KLEIN buttonbuddha@gmail.com 718-897-4746
subject: copyright info, Ruth Klein, Button Buddha
description: The back of a button featuring a contact sticker for artist Ruth Klein, dated 2006, with an email and a 718 area code phone number.

title: Brooklyn Parents For Peace
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Brooklyn
description: Colorful button with peace symbols and rainbows promoting the Brooklyn Parents for Peace organization.

title: None
subject: Cold War, US-USSR Relations, Peace
description: A button featuring a design that combines the United States and Soviet Union flags with a silhouette of St. Basil's Cathedral in the center. This type of button was commonly produced during the Reagan-Gorbachev summits of the late 1980s.

title: STOP THE EUROMISSILES OCT. 21-24
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Euromissiles, Peace Movement
description: This button was created to promote and commemorate the international days of protest held from October 21-24, 1983, against the deployment of U.S. Pershing II and cruise missiles in Western Europe. The graphic shows hands held up to block incoming missiles over a map of Europe.

title: VOTE BABY VOTE
subject: Voting Rights, Civil Rights, SNCC
description: A black circular button with white text that reads "VOTE BABY VOTE". This slogan was popularized by civil rights organizations like the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee (SNCC) in the mid-1960s to encourage voter registration and participation in the African American community.

title: WOMENS PEACE CAMP GREENHAM COMMON
subject: anti-nuclear, peace, feminism
description: A button for the Greenham Common Women's Peace Camp, established in 1981 to protest the decision of the British government to allow cruise missiles to be based at RAF Greenham Common.

title: STOP U.S.$ FOR SALVADORAN DEATH SQUADS
subject: El Salvador Civil War, US Foreign Policy, Human Rights
description: A grey pin-back button with red text reading "STOP U.S.$ FOR SALVADORAN DEATH SQUADS" superimposed over a tan handprint graphic. The button protests United States financial support for the Salvadoran government during its civil war.

title: don't let the dream die
subject: Civil Rights, Memorial, Martin Luther King Jr.
description: This button was produced by the Fellowship of Reconciliation following the assassination of Martin Luther King Jr. in 1968. It features a portrait of Dr. King and the text "don't let the dream die" as a call for the continuation of his civil rights work.

title: Paix
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Dove
description: Small blue button with a white dove and the French word for peace, 'Paix'.

title: U.S. HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR
subject: Anti-interventionism, Foreign Policy, El Salvador
description: This button features red text that reads "U.S. HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR" on a yellow background. The central image includes a red silhouette of the country of El Salvador with a black bird's claw reaching down from above. Buttons like this were produced by anti-war activists in the late 1970s and 1980s to protest U.S. military aid and involvement in the Salvadoran Civil War.

title: FMLN EL SALVADOR VENCERA
subject: Salvadoran Civil War, FMLN, Guerrilla Warfare
description: A button supporting the FMLN (Farabundo Mart� National Liberation Front) in El Salvador. It features an image of a revolutionary holding a rifle aloft over a map of the country, with the text 'EL SALVADOR VENCERA' which translates to 'El Salvador will win'.

title: WE'RE IN THIS TOGETHER
subject: Cold War, US-Soviet Relations, Peace
description: A yellow button featuring the Soviet and American flags juxtaposed, with the text 'WE'RE IN THIS TOGETHER' below. The button promotes cooperation and peace between the two superpowers during the Cold War era.

title: december 1986 Greenham
subject: Anti-nuclear, Feminism, Protest
description: A round yellow button featuring the text 'december 1986 Greenham' in green, with red interlinked female and peace symbols below. The button is bordered by a green chain-link fence pattern, representing the fence around the RAF Greenham Common base where women protested against nuclear weapons.

title: NUCLEAR-FREE ZONE
subject: nuclear energy, peace movement, activism
description: A button with a white background and black text reading 'NUCLEAR-FREE ZONE' in a circle around a central image. The image is a black radiation symbol (trefoil) on a yellow circle, covered by a red circle with a slash through it, signifying opposition to nuclear power and weapons.

title: SOCIAL WORKERS FOR PEACE AND NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT
subject: peace, nuclear disarmament, social workers
description: A white circular pinback button with blue text and graphics. It features the phrase "SOCIAL WORKERS FOR PEACE AND NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT" and a central graphic showing a peace dove with an olive branch over a bomb.

title: Say NO to Radioactive Waste
subject: Radioactive waste, Nuclear energy, Environmental activism
description: A pin-back button with the message 'Say NO to Radioactive Waste' written in red capital letters on a white background, with a small black and white graphic of a hand giving a thumbs down at the bottom.

title: IF YOU’VE SEEN ONE NUCLEAR WAR YOU’VE SEEN THEM ALL
subject: Anti-Nuclear War, Cold War, Nuclear Weapons Disarmament
description: This button features the text ‘If you’ve seen one nuclear war you’ve seen them all.’ In the center of the button is a graphic illustrating rubble and a pair of cockroaches, a common cultural reference to the idea that only cockroaches would survive a nuclear holocaust.

title: JEWS FOR NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT NEW JEWISH AGENDA • RAINBOW SIGN '83
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Jewish Activism, Peace Movement
description: A circular green button featuring black text. The center reads 'JEWS FOR NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT'. The bottom edge contains the text 'NEW JEWISH AGENDA • RAINBOW SIGN '83'. The button reflects the involvement of progressive Jewish groups in the anti-nuclear movement of the early 1980s.

title: FREE MAN NELSON MANDELA
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Nelson Mandela, Human Rights
description: A circular button calling for the release of Nelson Mandela from prison. The design features a portrait of Mandela centered over three horizontal stripes of black, green, and gold, which are the colors of the African National Congress (ANC). The text 'FREE MAN' is at the top and 'NELSON MANDELA' is at the bottom.

title: ATOMKRAFT? NEJ TACK
subject: Nuclear energy, Sweden, Environmentalism
description: A Swedish version of the iconic 'Smiling Sun' button, advocating against nuclear power. The phrase 'Atomkraft? Nej tack' translates to 'Nuclear power? No thanks'. It was a major symbol during the 1980 Swedish nuclear power referendum campaign.

title: ABOLISH NUKES! Abolition Now! www.abolitionnow.org
subject: Nuclear disarmament, Anti-nuclear movement, Abolition 2000
description: A political button featuring a large sunflower, the symbol of the global anti-nuclear movement Abolition 2000. The text calls for the abolition of nuclear weapons and provides the website URL for the campaign.

title: FARMS NOT ARMS
subject: Agriculture, Peace movement, Military spending
description: A button featuring a farm landscape with a red barn, yellow house, and cultivated fields, featuring the slogan 'FARMS NOT ARMS'. The slogan is a call to redirect government funding from military expenditures toward agricultural and social support.

title: less fission MORE VISION
subject: anti-nuclear, environment, energy
description: Yellow button with 'less fission' in black lowercase text and 'MORE VISION' in large, stylized letters filled with a landscape drawing of a sun and fields. It is a protest button from the anti-nuclear movement.

title: Alles im GRÜNEN Bereich?
subject: Nuclear power, Environmentalism, Green Party
description: A German political button for The Green Party (Die Grünen) featuring the phrase 'Alles im GRÜNEN Bereich?' (Is everything in the green zone?). The design includes a red 'X' over a nuclear power plant with sunflowers in the foreground, representing the party's anti-nuclear energy platform.

title: NO NUKES
subject: Anti-nuclear power, Environment, Sea turtle
description: A circular button with a blue grid background. The button features the slogan 'NO NUKES' in dark blue lettering and a graphic of a leatherback sea turtle.

title: ANC
subject: Anti-apartheid, South Africa, African National Congress
description: A button featuring the emblem of the African National Congress (ANC), which includes a spear, a shield, a wheel, and the ANC's tricolor flag of black, green, and gold.

title: end the war now HIROSHIMA NAGASAKI WEEK august 2-9
subject: Anti-War, Nuclear Weapons, Hiroshima/Nagasaki
description: A red and white circular button with text commemorating Hiroshima Nagasaki Week (August 2-9) and urging an end to the war, likely referencing the Vietnam War era.

title: Peace Symbol
subject: peace, anti-war, protest
description: A circular button featuring a black peace symbol over an orange background.

title: $$$$ for JOBS NOT BOMBS Solidarity Day - Sept. 19
subject: Labor, Peace, Protest
description: A red and yellow button from Solidarity Day on September 19, 1981. The button features the slogan 'Jobs Not Bombs' and four dollar signs, advocating for economic investment in employment rather than military spending during a mass protest rally organized by the AFL-CIO.

title: STOP THE PROFITS OF DOOM
subject: Anti-nuclear power, Corporate greed, Environmentalism
description: A black pin-back button featuring yellow text that reads 'STOP THE PROFITS OF DOOM' around the perimeter. In the center is a yellow graphic of an atom with a dollar sign ($) inside.

title: Stop It
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war, Protest
description: A circular button featuring stylized blue text that reads 'STOP IT' and is shaped like a falling bomb. The background consists of horizontal red and white stripes reminiscent of the American flag. This design was used as an anti-war protest symbol during the Vietnam War era.

title: I SURVIVED THREE MILE ISLAND I THINK
subject: Nuclear Power, Three Mile Island, Satire
description: A black button with white text that reads 'I SURVIVED THREE MILE ISLAND I THINK', referring to the nuclear accident at the Three Mile Island Nuclear Generating Station in Pennsylvania on March 28, 1979.

title: For Peace and Disarmament Put Canada First!
subject: Peace, Disarmament, Canada
description: A red button with black text and a graphic of a dove. The text reads 'For Peace and Disarmament Put Canada First!' advocating for Canadian neutrality and nuclear disarmament.

title: Stop Nuclear Investments TAKE IT TO WALL STREET October 28-29
subject: nuclear energy, anti-nuclear movement, divestment
description: Anti-nuclear protest button from the 'Take It to Wall Street' demonstrations held on October 28-29, 1979, in New York City, following the Three Mile Island accident.

title: STOP the CRUISE and PERSHING II MISSILES SENECA ARMY DEPOT OCT. 22-24
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Seneca Army Depot, Peace
description: A blue and white button featuring a peace dove, advocating against the deployment of Cruise and Pershing II missiles at the Seneca Army Depot during protests in October 1983.

title: © 1982 BULBUL-DONNELLY/COLT BUTTONS BOX 188 HAMPTON CT 06247
subject: activism, button manufacturing, feminist cartoons
description: The reverse side of a square pinback button produced in 1982 by Bulbul-Donnelly/Colt Buttons, a manufacturer known for social and political activism materials based in Hampton, Connecticut.

title: IF WE CAN RISK NUCLEAR WAR WE CAN RISK DISARMAMENT
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace movement, anti-nuclear
description: Square white button featuring a cartoon illustration of a person unraveling nuclear missiles into a pink knitted banner. The text on the banner reads "IF WE CAN RISK NUCLEAR WAR WE CAN RISK DISARMAMENT".

title: Long Live the Intifada
subject: Palestine, Resistance, Intifada
description: A white button with black text reading "Long Live the Intifada" surrounding a depiction of Handala, a symbol of Palestinian defiance created by cartoonist Naji al-Ali.

title: Peace Symbol, Cross, and Dove
subject: Peace, Christianity, Anti-war
description: A light blue button featuring a graphic that merges a peace symbol with a cross, with a white dove holding an olive branch in flight in front of it.

title: WHEN YOU'VE SEEN ONE ATOMIC WAR YOU'VE SEEN THEM ALL
subject: Anti-nuclear war, peace, disarmament
description: A round pink button with a white swirl pattern and black text that reads 'WHEN YOU'VE SEEN ONE ATOMIC WAR YOU'VE SEEN THEM ALL'. The slogan is a satirical play on a quote attributed to Spiro Agnew regarding city slums.

title: STOP NUCLEAR POWER
subject: Anti-nuclear, environmentalism, energy
description: A black button with white hand-drawn lettering that reads 'STOP NUCLEAR POWER'. This type of button was widely used in the United States during the anti-nuclear movement of the late 1970s and early 1980s.

title: paix CARAVANE DE LA PÉTITION pour la paix
subject: Peace movement, Nuclear disarmament, France
description: This French button promotes the 'Caravane de la pétition pour la paix' (Peace Petition Caravan). The caravan was an initiative by the French Peace Movement (Mouvement de la Paix) in the early 1980s, specifically around 1982, to gather support for nuclear disarmament during the Euromissiles crisis. The design features a stylized dove holding an olive branch, with its outline also forming the profile of a human face.

title: PUNCH 9 Harold Washington For-Chicago
subject: Mayoral Election, Chicago Politics, African American History
description: A campaign button for Harold Washington's 1983 Chicago mayoral campaign, featuring the slogan 'Punch 9' which referred to his position on the ballot.

title: FREE PALESTINE
subject: Palestine, Liberation, Activism
description: A political button advocating for Palestinian liberation, featuring a photo of a young person and the colors of the Palestinian flag with the slogan 'FREE PALESTINE'.

title: SUN POWER NOW!
subject: Solar Energy, Environmentalism, Renewable Energy
description: A light blue button with a stylized yellow sun and red rays, containing the text "SUN POWER NOW!" advocating for solar energy development.

title: Stop The Arms Race 1 in a MILLION
subject: anti-nuclear, disarmament, peace
description: A purple button with yellow text reading "Stop The Arms Race 1 in a MILLION" and a yellow star with the number 1, commemorating the June 12, 1982 anti-nuclear rally in Central Park, New York City.

title: A NUCLEAR-FREE EUROPE
subject: Nuclear disarmament, Peace movement, Europe
description: A political button from the 1980s advocating for a nuclear-free Europe. The button features yellow and red sections with black text and symbols, including two peace signs and a modified radiation symbol containing an exclamation point.

title: STOP THE U.S. WAR from the Middle East to El Salvador CISPES
subject: Anti-war, Central America, Middle East
description: Anti-war button from the early 1990s produced by the Committee in Solidarity with the People of El Salvador (CISPES). The button features gold and white text on a blue background, calling for an end to U.S. military involvement in both the Middle East (referring to the Gulf War) and El Salvador.

title: Take a chance: care LIFE & PEACE WEEK, 1981
subject: Peace, Religion, Family
description: A circular yellow button from 1981 featuring brown graphics. It depicts a stylized dove carrying an olive branch over a cross, with silhouettes of a man, woman, and child inside the dove. The text around the edge reads 'Take a chance: care' and 'LIFE & PEACE WEEK, 1981'.

title: COUNTDOWN ACID RAIN ENVIRONMENT ONTARIO
subject: Environment, Pollution, Acid Rain
description: A rectangular button produced by Environment Ontario to promote the 'Countdown Acid Rain' program, a government initiative launched in 1985 to drastically reduce sulfur dioxide emissions in the province by 1994. The button features a stopwatch graphic with years marked on the dial.

title: GREENPEACE
subject: Environmentalism, Peace, Activism
description: A circular button featuring a white dove carrying an olive branch, set against a concentric rainbow background. The word 'GREENPEACE' is written in white block letters at the bottom.

title: COALITION FOR A NON-NUCLEAR WORLD
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, environmental activism, pacifism
description: The reverse side of a pin-back button produced for the Coalition for a Non-Nuclear World, featuring the organization's address in Washington, D.C. and identifying Joel Deprez as the copyright holder for the design. The coalition was notably active in organizing the 1979 March on Washington against nuclear power and weapons.

title: MARCH FOR A NON-NUCLEAR WORLD APRIL 26-28, 1980
subject: Anti-nuclear, Peace, Environment
description: A green button with gold graphics and text promoting the March for a Non-Nuclear World held in Washington D.C. from April 26-28, 1980. The imagery includes a sun, a windmill, and a dove with an olive branch.

title: PEACE سلام PAIX 平和 МИР PAZ שלום
subject: peace, dove, anti-war
description: A green button featuring a white dove in the center, surrounded by the word 'peace' in seven different languages: English (PEACE), Arabic (سلام), French (PAIX), Chinese/Japanese (平和), Russian (МИР), Spanish (PAZ), and Hebrew (שלום).

title: STOP ALMELO + GRONAU
subject: anti-nuclear, protest, uranium enrichment
description: A pink button with black text reading 'STOP ALMELO + GRONAU' and a graphic of a missile over a stylized nuclear facility. This button expresses opposition to uranium enrichment plants located in Almelo, Netherlands, and Gronau, Germany.

title: PALESTINE: LET THE INTIFADA PAVE THE WAY FOR PEOPLE'S WAR
party: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
subject: Palestine, Intifada, Resistance
description: A square red button with English and Arabic text calling for the Intifada to lead to a people's war, featuring a militant and Leila Khaled with rifles.

title: END THE ARMS RACE NOT THE HUMAN RACE
subject: Peace, Disarmament, Activism
description: A white button featuring a blue circle in the center with a white silhouette of a dove. Around the perimeter, the text reads 'END THE ARMS RACE' at the top and 'NOT THE HUMAN RACE' at the bottom in a magenta-pink font.

title: BLACK by nature PROUD by choice
subject: Black pride, identity, pan-Africanism
description: A circular pinback button with the slogan "BLACK by nature PROUD by choice". The words are color-coded in white, green, yellow, and red against a dark background, reflecting Pan-African colors and the Black Pride movement.

title: CHOOSE PEACE
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Social activism
description: A white button featuring a blue line drawing of a dove carrying an olive branch, a universal symbol of peace. The text at the bottom is written in blue block letters and reads 'CHOOSE PEACE'.

title: PALESTINE TROOPS OUT NOW
subject: Palestine, Anti-war, Protest
description: A political protest button calling for the withdrawal of troops from Palestine. The design features a silhouette of a soldier with a rifle and incorporates the colors of the Palestinian flag (red, green, black, and white).

title: No Nukes
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, environmentalism, peace movement
description: A round white button with 'No Nukes' printed in green ink above a green line-drawing illustration of a mountain range and pine trees.

title: U.S. CUT DIPLOMATIC TIES WITH SOUTH AFRICA NCSAL
subject: Anti-Apartheid Movement, Foreign Policy, Activism
description: A circular green button featuring a black silhouette of the African continent with South Africa highlighted in red. The red text reads 'U.S. CUT DIPLOMATIC TIES WITH SOUTH AFRICA' above 'NCSAL', which likely stands for the Northern California Southern Africa Liberation Committee. This button was produced to advocate for US sanctions and diplomatic isolation of South Africa during the apartheid era.

title: Japanese Atomic Bombing by the United States Aug. 1945
subject: Atomic Bombing, Hiroshima, Nagasaki
description: The back of a button providing a chronological record of the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in August 1945, including times in both local Japanese time and Eastern Daylight Time. The button was manufactured by Gateways Unlimited in Washington, D.C.

title: Hiroshima / Nagasaki '45 N H '81 Lest We Forget First National H/N Commemoration Aug 5-9
subject: Hiroshima, Nagasaki, Anti-nuclear
description: A blue button featuring red 'N' and 'H' letters separated by a yellow lightning bolt. Text commemorates the 1945 atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki and advertises the First National H/N Commemoration event in August 1981.

title: End Israeli Apartheid
subject: Palestine, Apartheid, Human Rights
description: A white circular button featuring the text 'End Israeli Apartheid' in black bold letters. In the center is an illustration of Handala, a character created by Naji al-Ali that serves as a symbol of Palestinian national identity and resistance.

title: Full Employment Now Martin Luther King, Jr. Center for Social Change Jobs and Justice Jan. 15, 1977
subject: Civil rights, Labor rights, Economic justice
description: A white button featuring a black-and-white sketch of Martin Luther King, Jr. It advocates for 'Full Employment Now' and 'Jobs and Justice' on January 15, 1977, in association with the Martin Luther King, Jr. Center for Social Change and sponsored by McDonald's.

title: from Hiroshima to Harrisburg no nukes SPREAD THE WORD!
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Nuclear power, Three Mile Island accident
description: An anti-nuclear button featuring a stylized blue cooling tower emitting a mushroom cloud with the words 'no nukes'. Pink text around the border reads 'from Hiroshima to Harrisburg' and 'SPREAD THE WORD!'

title: Free South Africa VICTORY TO ANC & SWAPO
subject: Anti-Apartheid, South Africa, Liberation
description: A white circular button featuring red and black text. The top reads 'Free South Africa' in bold red font. Below is an illustration of two black arms with clenched fists, breaking free from shackles. The bottom text reads 'VICTORY TO ANC & SWAPO' with a smaller line mentioning 'US OUT OF SOUTHERN AFRICA NETWORK'.

title: ARRET DE TOUS LES ESSAIS NUCLEAIRES APPEL DES CENT
subject: Anti-nuclear, Peace movement, France
description: A green circular button featuring a white dove encircling a globe. The text translates to "Stop all nuclear tests" and "Call of the Hundred." This button represents L'Appel des Cent, a French anti-nuclear movement founded in 1982 that advocated for nuclear disarmament.

title: Free Palestine
subject: Palestine, Liberation, Activism
description: A white button featuring the Palestinian flag in the center with the words "Free" above it and "Palestine" below it in bold black text.

title: HELL NO WE WON'T GLOW
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, environmentalism, activism
description: A round pinback button featuring the slogan 'HELL NO WE WON'T GLOW' in white block letters against a horizontal rainbow background. The phrase is a popular anti-nuclear power protest slogan that gained prominence in the late 1970s. The rim of the button includes contact information for a post office box in Santa Cruz, California.

title: HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR NO MÁS VIETNAMS
subject: Anti-war movement, Central American solidarity, El Salvador Civil War
description: A political protest button from the early 1980s featuring a large hand reaching toward a map of Central America with El Salvador highlighted. It includes the slogans 'HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR' and 'NO MÁS VIETNAMS,' referencing the concern that U.S. involvement in El Salvador's civil war would lead to another Vietnam-like conflict.

title: ST. JOHN'S COLLEGE H.S. 1983 Cadets March for World Peace PEACE
subject: World Peace, High School, Activism
description: A light blue button featuring dark blue text and a graphic of a dove holding a 'PEACE' banner. It commemorates a march for world peace by the cadets of St. John's College High School in 1983.

title: HELP STOP THE BLOOD SHED IN PALESTINE ENOUGH
subject: Palestine, human rights, protest
description: Circular white pinback button with a central image of the Palestinian flag. A single drop of blood drips from the red triangle of the flag. Text on either side and below reads 'HELP STOP THE BLOOD SHED IN PALESTINE ENOUGH'.

title: LET THE SUN SHINE IN SUPPORT SOLAR ENERGY
subject: solar energy, environment, renewable energy
description: A political button advocating for solar energy featuring a stylized sun rising over the words 'SUPPORT SOLAR ENERGY' with 'LET THE SUN SHINE IN' arched over the top.

title: BREW NOT BOMBS
subject: anti-war, peace, activism
description: A black button with white text that reads 'BREW NOT BOMBS'. This slogan is a play on the 'Food Not Bombs' movement, typically used to advocate for spending on community and social goods rather than warfare.

title: CODEPINK WOMEN FOR PEACE www.codepinkalert.org
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Women's activism
description: A pink button with white text advocating for CODEPINK: Women for Peace, a grassroots organization founded in 2002 in response to the impending Iraq War.

title: Create Peace
subject: Peace, Dove, Anti-war
description: A circular button with a white dove carrying an olive branch, set against a background divided into a blue sky and green ground. The words 'Create Peace' are printed in white text at the top and bottom of the button.

title: STOP NUCLEAR POWER! SHOREHAM · JUNE 3RD SHAD Alliance
subject: Nuclear Power, Protest, SHAD Alliance
description: Button produced by the Sound-Hudson Against Atomic Development (SHAD Alliance) for an anti-nuclear protest at the Shoreham Nuclear Power Plant on June 3, 1979.

title: FREE SOUTH AFRICA END APARTHEID
subject: Apartheid, South Africa, Social Justice
description: A circular white button featuring a black and white portrait of Archbishop Desmond Tutu in the center. The text 'FREE SOUTH AFRICA' is printed along the top border and 'END APARTHEID' is printed along the bottom border in black capital letters.

title: ATOMFRIT HAV GREENPEACE
subject: Greenpeace, Nuclear-free, Environment
description: A blue square button with rounded corners featuring a yellow graphic of a bird over waves and the Danish text "ATOMFRIT HAV" (Nuclear-free sea) above the word "GREENPEACE". This button was part of a campaign by Greenpeace to promote a sea free of nuclear weapons and waste.

title: PEOPLE BEFORE PROFITS! NO NUKES! YOUTH AGAINST WAR & FASCISM
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Anti-war, Social activism
description: An orange button with black text reading 'PEOPLE BEFORE PROFITS! NO NUKES!' and 'YOUTH AGAINST WAR & FASCISM'. It features a graphic of an atom with a dollar sign in the center, representing a protest against nuclear power and weapons being prioritized for corporate profit over public safety.

title: EL SALVADOR - NICARAGUA ¡VENCER O MORIR!
subject: Revolution, Central America, Solidarity
description: A circular pin featuring revolutionary iconography for El Salvador and Nicaragua. The center displays a green stylized volcano with a red flame or sun at the peak, overlaid with symbols of worker-peasant-soldier alliance: a crossed wrench and shovel with a central upright rifle. The top border reads 'EL SALVADOR - NICARAGUA' on a black background, and the bottom border reads '¡VENCER O MORIR!' (To win or to die!) on a red background.

title: MAKE ONTARIO NUCLEAR WEAPONS FREE!
subject: Nuclear weapons, Peace movement, Disarmament
description: Square button with green and pink graphics. A green silhouette of Ontario is centered in a pink diamond. White text reads 'MAKE ONTARIO NUCLEAR WEAPONS FREE!'. This button was likely produced during the height of the anti-nuclear movement in Canada in the 1980s.

title: CND
subject: peace, nuclear disarmament, activism
description: A light blue button featuring a white peace sign above the letters "CND," representing the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament.

title: PEACE JUSTICE
subject: Peace, Justice, Activism
