title: PHIL WEISER ATTORNEY GENERAL
subject: Election, Attorney General, Colorado
description: Circular political campaign button for Phil Weiser's run for Attorney General of Colorado. The button is blue and black with white text and features a silhouette of mountains and the Colorado state logo.

title: Gregory COLL U.S. CONGRESS
subject: Maryland, US House, Republican
description: A sheet of two red and yellow rectangular campaign stickers for Gregory Coll, who ran as a Republican for Maryland's 8th Congressional District in the U.S. House of Representatives.

title: TOMMY HANSON FOR CONGRESS 5TH DISTRICT
subject: Campaign, Politics, Elections
description: A campaign sticker for Tommy Hanson, a Republican candidate for the U.S. House of Representatives in Illinois's 5th district.

title: MOVING COLORADO FORWARD
subject: Colorado, Election, Governor
description: A campaign sticker for John Hickenlooper's 2010 gubernatorial campaign in Colorado, featuring the slogan 'MOVING COLORADO FORWARD' and a graphic of mountains.

title: MARK HARMON FOR CONGRESS
subject: Election, Tennessee, Congress
description: Rectangular campaign button for Mark Harmon's 2018 run for the U.S. House of Representatives in Tennessee's 2nd district. The design features a silhouette of the Knoxville skyline including the Sunsphere and the three-star emblem from the Tennessee state flag.

title: MARK HARMON FOR CONGRESS
subject: Political campaign, Knoxville, Tennessee
description: Campaign sticker for Mark Harmon, a Democratic candidate for Tennessee's 2nd congressional district in 2022. The design features a Knoxville skyline above the text and the three stars from the Tennessee state flag incorporated into the name.

title: KATIE BRITT US SENATE ALABAMA FIRST
subject: US Senate, Alabama, Election
description: A campaign button for Katie Britt's 2022 U.S. Senate run in Alabama, featuring a red silhouette of the state and the slogan 'Alabama First'.

title: Pro-MARY MARY PELTOLA for CONGRESS
subject: Mary Peltola, US House, Alaska
description: A campaign button for Mary Peltola's 2022 run for the US House of Representatives in Alaska. The button features the text 'Pro-MARY' in large font, a multicolored horizontal bar, and 'MARY PELTOLA for CONGRESS' in smaller text below.

title: BENNET FOR COLORADO
subject: Senate election, Colorado, Michael Bennet
description: A campaign sticker for Michael Bennet's 2016 U.S. Senate re-election campaign in Colorado, featuring his name in white and the phrase "FOR COLORADO" in red against a blue background.

title: MAGGIE HASSAN U.S. SENATOR · FOR NEW HAMPSHIRE TEXT WIN TO 21091
subject: Senator, Election, New Hampshire
description: A campaign sticker for Maggie Hassan's re-election campaign for U.S. Senator from New Hampshire. The sticker features her name, an outline of the state, and a shortcode for text-based campaign communications.

title: MAGGIE HASSAN U.S. SENATOR · FOR NEW HAMPSHIRE
subject: elections; senate; New Hampshire
description: A rectangular campaign sticker for Maggie Hassan's 2022 U.S. Senate re-election campaign in New Hampshire, featuring her name, the office, a map of the state, and a campaign text-to-join short code.

title: REBEKAH JONES FOR CONGRESS
subject: Political Campaign, Florida, Rebekah Jones
description: A campaign sticker for Rebekah Jones, who ran as a Democrat for the U.S. House of Representatives in Florida's 1st congressional district in the 2022 election.

title: KATIE BRITT US SENATE ALABAMA FIRST
subject: US Senate, Alabama, Election
description: A blue circular campaign button for Katie Britt's 2022 U.S. Senate run in Alabama. It features her name in white, an orange outline of the state of Alabama, and the slogans "US SENATE" and "ALABAMA FIRST".

title: MICHELS FOR GOVERNOR
subject: Gubernatorial election, Wisconsin politics, Republican Party
description: A political button supporting Tim Michels for Governor of Wisconsin in the 2022 election.

title: KAT for CONGRESS NO MORE CHICKEN SH*T POLITICIANS
subject: Kat Swift, Green Party, 2010 Election
description: A campaign sticker or button for Kat Swift, the Green Party candidate for Texas's 21st congressional district in 2010. The design features a drawing of a chicken and the provocative slogan 'No More Chicken Sh*t Politicians'.

title: CATHERINE CORTEZ MASTO FOR SENATE
subject: US Senate, Election, Nevada
description: A rectangular campaign sticker or card for Catherine Cortez Masto's 2016 run for the U.S. Senate representing Nevada. The design includes her name in blue and orange, the words 'FOR SENATE' in blue, and an orange outline of the state of Nevada.

title: REAL REPRESENTATION DANIELLE HAWK FOR CONGRESS DISTRICT 3 daniellehawk.com
subject: LGBTQ+, Election, Florida
description: Campaign sticker for Danielle Hawk, a Democratic candidate for Florida's 3rd Congressional District in 2022, featuring a rainbow-colored map of Florida.

title: RE-ELECT DEMOCRAT JAMIE RASKIN FOR CONGRESS
subject: Maryland politics, Congressional elections, Jamie Raskin
description: A political campaign button for the re-election of Jamie Raskin, a Democrat from Maryland, to the U.S. House of Representatives. The button is blue with white and red lettering and features a star in the middle of his last name.

title: Keep your dreams BIG and your government small KAT for CONGRESS
subject: Small Government, Congress, Campaign
description: A campaign sticker for Kat Cammack's congressional campaign, featuring a graphic of the U.S. Capitol dome and her slogan 'Keep your dreams BIG and your government small'. Cammack was first elected to Florida's 3rd congressional district in 2020.

title: TEAM KAT
subject: Kat Cammack, Florida, Congressional Election
description: Campaign material for Kat Cammack's run for the U.S. House of Representatives in Florida. It features the text 'TEAM KAT' with the letter 'A' represented by a Florida map with stars and stripes.

title: Dr. Asif MAHMOOD FOR CONGRESS
subject: Election, Healthcare, Physician
description: A circular blue campaign button for Dr. Asif Mahmood, a Democrat who ran for the US House of Representatives in California's 40th district in 2022. The button features a white stethoscope illustration.

title: Pro-FISH MARY PELTOLA for CONGRESS
subject: fishing, environment, campaign
description: A blue campaign button for Mary Peltola's 2022 run for the U.S House of Representatives from Alaska, featuring a salmon illustration and the words 'Pro-FISH'.

title: BENNET FOR COLORADO
subject: Michael Bennet, US Senate, Colorado
description: Campaign sticker for Michael Bennet's 2010 US Senate election in Colorado, featuring white and red text over a stylized blue mountain background.

title: WE THE PEOPLE KAT FOR CONGRESS
subject: Election, Florida, Alligator
description: Campaign sticker for Kat Cammack's 2020 run for Florida's 3rd congressional district, featuring an alligator and the pattern of the Florida state flag.

title: JARED POLIS DIANNE PRIMAVERA for COLORADO
subject: Colorado, Election, Governor
description: A rectangular political sticker for the 2018 Colorado gubernatorial campaign of Jared Polis and his running mate Dianne Primavera. The design features a stylized mountain logo.

title: TAKE BACK AMERICA Elect Tom ZMICH for US Congress
subject: Tom Zmich, US Congress, New York
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign sticker for Tom Zmich's 2020 campaign for the United States House of Representatives in New York's 6th congressional district.

title: BIDEN / WARREN
subject: Presidential Election, 2020, Democratic Party
description: A campaign sticker for Elizabeth Warren with a Joe Biden logo sticker placed over the letters 'WAR' in 'WARREN', signifying her supporters' shift to Biden after she withdrew from the race. The sticker features the campaign website WWW.ELIZABETHWARREN.COM at the bottom.

title: JGS jennygarciasharon.com Texas Bold Texas Strong
subject: Campaign, Texas, US House
description: This oval campaign sticker for Republican Jenny Garcia Sharon features her initials 'JGS' in large red text set against a background of the American flag. It includes her campaign website and the slogan 'Texas Bold Texas Strong'. Jenny Garcia Sharon was a candidate for the U.S. House of Representatives representing Texas's 35th District in 2020 and 2022.

title: BASLER FOR CONGRESS
subject: Elections, Politics, Ohio
description: A red circular campaign button for Richard Basler's 1974 run for the United States House of Representatives in Ohio's 11th district, shown here mounted on a white paper cutout of the state of Ohio.

title: PENNY RONNING U.S. CONGRESS Montana's Second Congressional District People Over Politics
subject: Montana politics, Congressional election, Campaign sticker
description: Blue rectangular campaign sticker for Penny Ronning's 2022 run for the U.S. House of Representatives in Montana's 2nd District. Features the slogan 'People Over Politics' and three stars.

title: EMANUEL Cleaver, II U.S. CONGRESS
subject: Emanuel Cleaver, Missouri, U.S. House
description: Campaign button for Emanuel Cleaver, II's candidacy for the United States House of Representatives from Missouri's 5th district. Cleaver, a Democrat and former mayor of Kansas City, was first elected to Congress in 2004.

title: CONOR HALBLEIB CONGRESS
subject: Politics, Campaign, Election
description: A blue and white campaign sticker for Conor Halbleib. While the sticker states 'CONGRESS', Conor Halbleib is a Democratic politician from Louisville, Kentucky, who was a candidate for the Kentucky State Senate District 26 in the 2022 election cycle.

title: Pro-FISH Pro-FAMILY Pro-JOBS Pro-CHOICE
subject: Fisheries, Reproductive rights, Alaska politics
description: Red campaign button for Mary Peltola's 2022 campaign for Alaska's at-large congressional district, listing her campaign platform: 'Pro-FISH Pro-FAMILY Pro-JOBS Pro-CHOICE'.

title: H-BLOCK-P.O.W.-LONG KESH
subject: Irish Hunger Strike, Long Kesh, H-Block
description: Green button featuring a stylized face of Bobby Sands with the text "H-BLOCK-P.O.W.-LONG KESH" in black. This refers to the protest for political status for Irish republican prisoners at the Maze Prison.

title: TROOPS OUT NOW
subject: Northern Ireland, Withdrawal, Military
description: Round green button featuring a black silhouette of Great Britain as a figure swinging a club towards Ireland. The text 'TROOPS OUT NOW' appears along the bottom edge. This button is associated with the Troops Out Movement, a British organization formed in 1974 advocating for the withdrawal of British troops from Northern Ireland.

title: FREE MICHAEL O'ROURKE
subject: Irish Republicanism, Prisoners' rights, Immigration
description: A green button with white text advocating for the release of Michael O'Rourke, an Irish republican who was arrested in Philadelphia in 1979 and held in US federal custody for several years while fighting deportation.

title: ARM THE IRA
subject: Irish Republican Army, The Troubles, Irish Republicanism
description: A white pin-back button featuring a green shamrock with white stars forming the 'Starry Plough' constellation, with the text 'ARM THE IRA' written in green above the shamrock.

title: IRA IRISH NORTHERN AID
subject: IRA, Ireland, NORAID
description: A circular button with a gold background and green text. 'IRA' is printed in large capital letters in the center, with 'IRISH NORTHERN AID' curved along the bottom. Irish Northern Aid, also known as NORAID, was a US-based organization founded in 1970 to support the Irish republican movement during the Troubles.

title: I. R. A.
subject: Irish Republican Army, Nationalism, Ireland
description: A circular button featuring the acronym 'I. R. A.' for the Irish Republican Army, printed vertically over a background of the Irish tricolor flag.

title: SUPPORT IRISH POLITICAL PRISONERS H-BLOCK & ARMAGH COMM.
subject: Political Prisoners, Hunger Strikes, Irish Republicanism
description: A political button produced by the National H-Block/Armagh Committee to support Irish Republican prisoners in Northern Ireland during the protests for political status in the late 1970s and early 1980s. The central graphic depicts three stylized faces representing the prisoners.

title: VICTORY TO THE I.R.A.
subject: Irish Republicanism, Northern Ireland, IRA
description: A blue button featuring the Starry Plough constellation in white. The text "VICTORY TO THE I.R.A." is printed in white around the top and bottom edges. The Starry Plough is a traditional symbol of the Irish Citizen Army and Irish socialist republicanism.

title: ÓGRA SHINN FÉIN
subject: Irish Republicanism, Youth Politics, Sinn Féin
description: A button for Ógra Shinn Féin, the youth wing of the Irish political party Sinn Féin. The central image depicts a stylized petrol bomb containing an Easter Lily in its flame, symbols associated with Irish republicanism.

title: Sinn Féin
subject: Irish Nationalism, Political Party, Ireland
description: Round button featuring the name "Sinn Féin" with large stylized letters 'S' and 'F' in black, over a green silhouette map of Ireland, on a white background with green and orange concentric circles.

title: Sinn Féin
subject: Ireland, Sinn Féin, Political Party
description: A round pin featuring a black SF monogram and the text "Sinn Féin" over a green map of Ireland on a white background.

title: IRISH CIVIL RIGHTS
subject: Civil rights, Northern Ireland, Irish republicanism
description: Green button with white text reading "IRISH CIVIL RIGHTS" around a central image of a white Easter lily with an orange center. The Easter lily is a symbol used by Irish republicans to commemorate those who died for Irish independence.

title: NATIONAL ASSOCIATION FOR IRISH FREEDOM SAOIRSE
subject: Irish Republicanism, Northern Ireland, Starry Plough
description: A green circular button featuring the yellow 'Starry Plough' symbol. The text 'NATIONAL ASSOCIATION FOR IRISH FREEDOM' arcs around the top, and 'SAOIRSE' (the Irish word for freedom) is printed at the bottom. This button is associated with the National Association for Irish Freedom, an American support group for Irish republicanism active during the late 1960s and 1970s.

title: FREEDOM FOR IRELAND SINN FÉIN THE WORKERS' PARTY
subject: Irish republicanism, Socialism, Starry Plough
description: A green pin-back button featuring the Starry Plough symbol in white and black. The text on the button reads 'FREEDOM FOR IRELAND' and 'SINN FÉIN THE WORKERS' PARTY'.

title: FREEDOM FOR IRELAND
subject: Irish independence, Nationalism, Northern Ireland
description: White circular button with the words 'FREEDOM FOR IRELAND' in green lettering. In the center are two colored squares, one green and one orange, echoing the Irish tricolor flag.

title: TROUPES BRITANNIQUES HORS D'IRLANDE!! LES SOCIALISTES INTERNATIONAUX
subject: Ireland, Socialism, Anti-war
description: Green button featuring a map of Ireland and Great Britain with a silhouette of a riot police officer holding a baton over the British mainland. The French text translates to "British Troops Out of Ireland!!" and identifies the group "The International Socialists." It includes a raised fist icon at the bottom.

title: AD HOC COMMITTEE FOR IRISH HUMAN RIGHTS END BRITISH TERROR IN IRELAND 212-338-0031
subject: Irish human rights, Northern Ireland, The Troubles
description: This pin-back button was produced by the New York-based Ad Hoc Committee for Irish Human Rights. It advocates for an end to British military and police presence in Northern Ireland during 'The Troubles,' featuring a green shamrock with blood dripping from its center.

title: IRISH NORTHERN AID COMMITTEE UNITE IRELAND
subject: Irish Nationalism, Unification, NORAID
description: This button was produced by the Irish Northern Aid Committee (NORAID), an American organization that provided support for Irish republicanism during the Troubles. It features a map of Ireland with Northern Ireland highlighted in orange and the message 'Unite Ireland'.

title: SMASH H-BLOCK! Political status now
subject: Hunger strike, Irish Republicanism, H-Block
description: This button was produced as part of the campaign to secure political status for Irish Republican prisoners held in the H-Blocks of HM Prison Maze in Northern Ireland. The protest, which began with the blanket protest in 1976, escalated into hunger strikes in 1980 and 1981, most notably led by Bobby Sands. The button features a bearded man with a raised fist, a symbol of defiance and solidarity.

title: UNITE IRELAND IRISH NORTHERN AID
subject: Ireland, Nationalism, Republicanism
description: A green pin-back button with the text 'UNITE IRELAND' in large white block letters, and 'IRISH NORTHERN AID' in smaller white letters at the bottom. This button was produced by the Irish Northern Aid Committee (NORAID), an American-based organization that raised funds for the Irish republican cause during The Troubles.

title: UNITY AGAINST INTERNMENT Irish Anti-Internment Coalition
subject: Northern Ireland, Internment, Civil Rights
description: A political protest button issued by the Irish Anti-Internment Coalition. It features the text 'UNITY AGAINST INTERNMENT' in green and depicts crossed lines of barbed wire in orange and green, symbolizing the opposition to the policy of internment without trial introduced in Northern Ireland in 1971.

title: T.F.U.I.
subject: Irish reunification, trade unionism, labor activism
description: This button features a green map of Ireland with the text 'T.F.U.I.' below it, which stands for 'Trade Unionists for a United Ireland'. This organization was active in the late 1970s and 1980s, advocating for the reunification of Ireland through the lens of the trade union and labor movement.

title: IRELAND IS ONE COUNTRY
subject: Ireland, Nationalism, Reunification
description: Circular button with three horizontal stripes of green, white, and orange, mimicking the Irish flag. The text reads 'IRELAND IS ONE COUNTRY' across the stripes.

title: IRELAND england's vietnam
subject: The Troubles, Ireland, Vietnam War
description: A political button comparing the British military presence in Northern Ireland to the Vietnam War. It features a red map of Ireland and a black silhouette of a hand holding an AK-47 on a green background.

title: PUTTING THE COUNTRY FIRST FG FINE GAEL
subject: Ireland, Fine Gael, general election
description: A campaign button for the Fine Gael political party in Ireland, featuring their 1981 general election slogan 'Putting the Country First'.

title: Give Ireland a Hand Boycott British Goods
subject: Irish independence, Boycott, British-Irish relations
description: A button advocating for Irish independence and a boycott of British goods. The image shows a map of Ireland with Northern Ireland colored red and the rest green. A hand, with a cuff in the colors of the Irish flag, is removing the red part of the map. A sleeve with the Union Jack is being held back.

title: END BRITISH TORTURE IN IRELAND
subject: The Troubles, Ireland, Human Rights
description: A political protest button featuring the text 'END BRITISH TORTURE IN IRELAND' in white and red against a black background. It likely refers to the interrogation techniques used by British security forces in Northern Ireland during the early 1970s which led to international legal proceedings.

title: TROOPS OUT NOW!
subject: The Troubles, Northern Ireland, British Military
description: A green and black pinback button featuring the text "TROOPS OUT NOW!" below an illustration where the island of Great Britain is depicted as a riot police officer with a baton and shield, standing next to the island of Ireland. The button represents the campaign for the withdrawal of British troops from Northern Ireland during the Troubles.

title: brits out !
subject: Irish Republicanism, Northern Ireland, Decolonization
description: This green button features the slogan 'brits out !' in black text at the bottom. Above the text is a black graphic parodying the 'Keep Britain Tidy' logo, depicting a figure throwing a British soldier in riot gear into a bin labeled 'EIRE'.

title: REMEMBER BOBBY SANDS BRITISH OUT OF IRELAND YSA
subject: Irish republicanism, Bobby Sands, hunger strike
description: A button with vertical green, white, and orange stripes representing the Irish flag. It features black text that reads "REMEMBER BOBBY SANDS" along the top edge, "BRITISH OUT OF IRELAND" in the center, and "YSA" (for the Young Socialist Alliance) at the bottom. The button memorializes Bobby Sands, an Irish republican who died during a hunger strike in 1981.

title: IRELAND 32
subject: Irish reunification, United Ireland, Irish nationalism
description: A circular button featuring the Irish tricolor (green, white, and orange) and an outline map of Ireland. The text 'IRELAND 32' refers to the political aim of reuniting all 32 counties of Ireland into a single state.

title: © COPYRIGHT 1996 PRODUCTS OF VISION, 14417 CHASE ST. #166 PANORAMA CITY, CA 91402 (818) 894-9149
subject: Manufacturing, 1996, Buttons
description: The reverse side of a circular button featuring a manufacturer's sticker for 'Products of Vision' in Panorama City, California, dated 1996.

title: [Prohibition sign over Union Jack]
subject: Northern Ireland, Irish Republicanism, Anti-British
description: A button featuring a green universal prohibition sign (a circle with a diagonal slash) over the Union Jack, the flag of the United Kingdom. This imagery was commonly used by Irish Republican groups to express 'Brits Out' sentiment during the Troubles in Northern Ireland.

title: off!
subject: Anti-police, Civil Rights, Black Panther Party
description: A circular green button featuring the word 'off!' in stylized black script. It depicts two black silhouettes: one of a pig dressed in a police uniform holding a baton and another smaller pig figure, referencing the Black Panther Party slogan 'Off the pig'.

title: There's no excuse WIFE ASSAULT It is a Crime
subject: domestic violence, social awareness, law
description: A pink diamond-shaped button with a black band produced by the Ontario government in the 1980s as part of a public awareness campaign stating that wife assault is a crime.

title: Fight the Attacks No Turning Back! INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY 1990
subject: Feminism, Women's rights, Activism
description: An oval red button featuring three white brush-stroke banners with the slogans "Fight the Attacks" and "No Turning Back!" in black text. Along the bottom edge, the text "INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY 1990" is printed in white.

title: Wife Assault No Man Has the Right
subject: domestic violence, women's rights, public awareness
description: Rectangular sticker from the 1980s Ontario government campaign 'No Man Has the Right' aimed at preventing wife assault. It features a red prohibition symbol over the words 'WIFE ASSAULT'.

title: COYOTE MY ASS IS MINE!
subject: sex worker rights, COYOTE, bodily autonomy
description: This button was produced by the sex workers' rights organization COYOTE (Cast Off Your Old Tired Ethics), which was founded by Margo St. James in San Francisco in 1973. The slogan "My Ass Is Mine!" was a prominent rallying cry for the decriminalization of prostitution and the assertion of individual rights over one's own body.

title: COYOTE! MY ASS IS MINE...
subject: Sex Workers Rights, Feminism, COYOTE
description: A button produced by COYOTE (Call Off Your Old Tired Ethics), an organization advocating for sex workers' rights founded in 1973 by Margo St. James. The button features a graphic of female anatomy and a slogan emphasizing bodily autonomy.

title: Oh, so that explains the difference in our salaries!
subject: pay equity, feminism, gender equality
description: A yellow button featuring a black and white illustration of a male and female toddler looking into their diapers with the caption 'Oh, so that explains the difference in our salaries!', satirizing the gender pay gap.

title: STOP RAPE
subject: Sexual Violence Prevention, Women's Rights, Social Activism
description: A circular button featuring a red hand symbol with the words 'STOP RAPE' in white text across the middle. This pinback was likely produced as part of the anti-rape and sexual assault awareness movements that gained momentum during the 1970s and 1980s.

title: INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY MARCH 5, 1983
subject: International Women's Day, Feminism, Activism
description: This button commemorates International Women's Day, dated March 5, 1983. It depicts an illustration of a woman in a red shirt holding a large purple flag.

title: In commemoration of the 14 women killed in Montreal, December 6, 1989
subject: Montreal Massacre, Violence against women, École Polytechnique
description: A commemorative button with a red rose, created in memory of the fourteen victims of the massacre at École Polytechnique in Montreal on December 6, 1989. The text is in both English and French.

title: "IF I CAN'T DANCE..." I DON'T WANT TO BE PART OF YOUR REVOLUTION Emma Goldman
subject: Anarchism, Feminism, Revolution
description: A button featuring a portrait of Emma Goldman and a famous paraphrased quote attributed to her: "If I can't dance, I don't want to be part of your revolution."

title: ANOTHER MAN AGAINST VIOLENCE AGAINST WOMEN
subject: violence against women, men's movement, feminism
description: A circular button divided horizontally; the top half is white with black text that reads "ANOTHER MAN AGAINST" and the bottom half is purple with white text that reads "VIOLENCE AGAINST WOMEN".

title: KEEP YOUR LAWS OFF MY BODY
subject: abortion rights, reproductive rights, bodily autonomy
description: A button with blue text on a light background reading 'KEEP YOUR LAWS OFF MY BODY'. This slogan emerged as a prominent rallying cry for the reproductive rights and feminist movements in the United States during the late 1960s and early 1970s.

title: TRIBUNAL INTERNATIONAL DES CRIMES CONTRE LA FEMME PALAIS DES CONGRES BRUXELLES 4-8 MARS 76
subject: feminism, international law, women's rights
description: A button promoting the International Tribunal on Crimes Against Women, which took place in Brussels, Belgium, in March 1976.

title: FIGHTING RACISM AND SEXISM TOGETHER! INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY MARCH 7, 1987
subject: Feminism, Anti-racism, International Women's Day
description: A square blue button featuring a series of pink and white horizontal stripes. In the center is a circle representing the head of a Venus symbol, containing stylized figures of four people of diverse backgrounds and physical abilities, including one in a wheelchair. The text around the symbol reads 'FIGHTING RACISM AND SEXISM TOGETHER!' and at the bottom 'INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY MARCH 7, 1987'.

title: A SINGLE RAPE VIOLATES ALL WOMEN
subject: feminism, sexual violence, women's rights
description: A round blue button with gold text that reads 'A SINGLE RAPE VIOLATES ALL WOMEN' and a gold Venus symbol on the left side.

title: Action Coalition for the ERA vote ERA '76 Massachusetts Referendum
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Women's Rights, Political Campaign
description: A blue button with white lettering that reads 'Action Coalition for the ERA vote ERA '76 Massachusetts Referendum'. This button was part of the campaign to pass a state-level Equal Rights Amendment in Massachusetts, which successfully passed on November 2, 1976.

title: NEVER ANOTHER BATTERED WOMAN
subject: Domestic Violence, Women's Rights, Feminism
description: A black and white button advocating against domestic violence. It features the text 'NEVER ANOTHER BATTERED WOMAN' in a semi-circle at the top. The central graphic shows the face and shoulders of a woman with a jagged lightning bolt-shaped crack running vertically through the middle of her face.

title: Harcèlement Sexuel Pas Question!
subject: sexual harassment, labor rights, feminism
description: A green button with pink text that reads 'Harcèlement Sexuel Pas Question!' (Sexual Harassment No Way!). The bottom edge includes the name of the organization 'Au Bas de l'Echelle - Rank and File'. This button was part of a major campaign launched in the early 1980s by the Montreal-based non-profit Au Bas de l'échelle to raise awareness about sexual harassment in the workplace, particularly for non-unionized workers.

title: Women Unite to Fight racism sexism economic inequality INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY TORONTO, 1988
subject: feminism, racism, economic inequality
description: White button with pink, purple, and teal text and graphics produced for International Women's Day 1988 in Toronto. The graphics show three people holding streamers that lead to a Venus symbol containing a globe.

title: WOMEN'S POLITICAL CAUCUS
subject: Women's Rights, Political Activism, Feminism
description: A red button for the Women's Political Caucus featuring a white graphic of the Statue of Liberty holding a ballot box instead of a torch, symbolizing the power of women's votes.

title: WOMEN UNITE TAKE BACK THE NIGHT
subject: feminism, sexual violence, activism
description: A circular purple button with white text reading 'WOMEN UNITE TAKE BACK THE NIGHT' and a graphic of two hands joined to look like a bird. The button represents the Take Back the Night movement, which began in the 1970s to protest sexual violence.

title: E.R.A. YES
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Women's Rights, Feminism
description: A round yellow button with black text reading 'E.R.A. YES', advocating for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment to the United States Constitution during the 1970s and early 1980s.

title: DEMS FOR ERA
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Women's Rights, Democratic Party
description: A circular red button featuring the text 'DEMS FOR ERA' in white. This button indicates the support of members of the Democratic Party for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA).

title: E.R.A. in '76! Vote Socialist Workers
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Women's Rights, Socialist Workers Party
description: This light blue button features a white female symbol with the text 'E.R.A. in '76!' inside and 'Vote Socialist Workers' to the left. It advocates for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment and supports the Socialist Workers Party in the 1976 election.

title: ERA. All people are created equal.
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Feminism, Women's Rights
description: A white button featuring the slogan "ERA. All people are created equal." in blue and red text, promoting the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment.

title: People of Faith FOR ERA
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, religion, feminism
description: Yellow and blue button advocating for the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA) on behalf of religious individuals and organizations.

title: Lesbian Rights are Women's Rights
subject: lesbian rights, feminism, civil rights
description: A circular purple button with white text that reads "Lesbian Rights are Women's Rights." In the center is an inverted white triangle containing the logo for the National Organization for Women (NOW).

title: LET'S FACE IT... WOMEN ARE SMARTER
subject: Feminism, Women's Rights, Gender
description: A circular pink pin-back button featuring two black silhouettes of women's profiles facing one another. The text 'LET'S FACE IT...' is printed in black between the faces. Below the silhouettes, the word 'WOMEN' is printed in large purple block letters, and underneath that, 'ARE SMARTER' is printed in black.

title: WOMEN UNITE TAKE BACK THE NIGHT
subject: Feminism, Women's Rights, Safety
description: A white circular pinback button for the Take Back the Night movement. It features black text reading "WOMEN UNITE" and "TAKE BACK THE NIGHT" around a central graphic of two female silhouettes kneeling back-to-back in grass under a crescent moon and star. The artwork includes a small signature and the date '80.

title: SUPPORT YOUR LOCAL FEMINIST
subject: feminism, women's rights, activism
description: A round brown button with the slogan "SUPPORT YOUR LOCAL FEMINIST" printed in white block lettering across three lines.

title: A$P ALLIANCE FOR THE SAFETY OF PROSTITUTES
subject: Sex worker rights, Prostitution, Advocacy
description: A button for the Alliance for the Safety of Prostitutes (ASP), a sex worker rights advocacy group founded in Philadelphia in 1980. The logo features a stylized poppy flower and the acronym A$P.

title: International Women's Tribunal October 21-22 New York WONAAC
subject: Reproductive Rights, Feminism, Activism
description: A pinback button for the International Women's Tribunal, which took place in New York City on October 21-22, 1972. The event was organized by the Women's National Abortion Action Coalition (WONAAC) to advocate for reproductive freedom and women's rights.

title: WOMEN'S LIBERATION
subject: feminism, women's rights, social movements
description: A tan circular pin featuring a dark brown Venus symbol with a raised fist inside. The text 'WOMEN'S LIBERATION' is printed in an arc at the bottom. This logo was a prominent symbol of the second-wave feminist movement in the late 1960s and 1970s.

title: STOP THE WAR AGAINST WOMEN!
subject: Women's rights, Feminism, Social activism
description: A square advocacy button featuring the slogan 'STOP THE WAR AGAINST WOMEN!' split between a white top half with red text and a black bottom half with white text. A small union bug with the number 461 is at the bottom.

title: WAGES FOR HOUSEWORK
subject: Feminism, Labor, Housework
description: A white and red button advocating for the Wages for Housework movement. It features the Venus symbol with a hand grasping paper currency inside, surrounded by the text 'WAGES FOR HOUSEWORK'. The currency depicted includes symbols for both dollars and pounds, reflecting the international nature of the campaign launched in 1972.

title: "FAILURE IS IMPOSSIBLE." Susan B. Anthony
subject: Women's Rights, Suffrage, Feminism
description: A yellow button with red lettering featuring Susan B. Anthony's famous quote, 'Failure is impossible.' This quote was originally from her final public speech in 1906 regarding the women's suffrage movement and was later adopted as a slogan by modern feminist movements in the 20th century.

title: DON'T BUY GRAPES
subject: boycott, labor, grapes
description: The reverse side of a United Farm Workers (UFW) 'Don't Buy Grapes' boycott button. The back features a metal pin, a union bug from the Allied Printing Trades Council, and a handwritten archival accession mark '1969 ERA 576'.

title: W76 Win With Women
subject: women in politics, feminism, elections
description: White circular button with blue text 'W76 Win With Women'. The stylized 'W' and '76' represent a 1976 campaign focused on electing women to political office, likely affiliated with the National Women's Political Caucus.

title: A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO WORK
subject: Women's rights, Employment, Feminism
description: A pink circular button with the text 'A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO WORK' in red. Behind the main text, smaller text repeats the words 'JUNE 21' and 'DEMONSTRATE' in a circular pattern, referring to a national demonstration held in London in 1980.

title: 1975 WHY NOT! INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S YEAR
subject: International Women's Year, Feminism, Women's rights
description: A red button with white text commemorating International Women's Year in 1975. The phrase "WHY NOT!" is prominently displayed in the center, with the letter 'O' replaced by a female symbol. The year "1975" is at the top, and "INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S YEAR" is written along the bottom edge.

title: MEN can stop RAPE
subject: Sexual assault, Activism, Feminism
description: A white button with the slogan 'MEN can stop RAPE' printed on it. The words 'MEN' and 'RAPE' are in black, while 'can stop' is in red script. This button was used in awareness campaigns to involve men in the prevention of sexual violence.

title: PEACE NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN
subject: Peace, Feminism, Activism
description: A circular button with a yellow background. The word 'PEACE' is prominently displayed in black, overlapping a stylized female gender symbol which is the logo of the National Organization for Women. Below it, 'NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN' is printed in black text.

title: PORN IS THE THEORY RAPE IS THE PRACTICE
subject: Feminism, Anti-pornography movement, Sexual violence
description: A circular tan button featuring the red-printed slogan 'PORN IS THE THEORY RAPE IS THE PRACTICE'. This phrase was coined by feminist author Robin Morgan in her 1974 essay and became a prominent slogan for the anti-pornography feminist movement during the late 1970s and early 1980s.

title: NOVEMBER 12th Mobilize FOR WOMEN'S LIVES ACROSS THE USA & WASHINGTON, DC
subject: Abortion Rights, Women's Rights, Protest
description: This button was created for the 'Mobilize for Women's Lives' rallies held on November 12, 1989. Organized primarily by the National Organization for Women (NOW), these nationwide demonstrations advocated for reproductive freedom and abortion rights following the Supreme Court's decision in Webster v. Reproductive Health Services.

title: 59¢
subject: Gender pay gap, Women's rights, University of Illinois
description: A button featuring '59¢' with a diagonal line through it, superimposed over a light blue University of Illinois 'I' logo. The '59¢' with a slash was a common symbol used in the 1980s to protest the gender pay gap, specifically the statistic that women earned 59 cents for every dollar men earned. This specific button likely represents a local campaign or protest at the University of Illinois.

title: 1975 INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY MARCH 8
subject: Women's rights, Feminism, International Women's Day
description: A pink and red button commemorating International Women's Day on March 8, 1975, featuring a stylized multi-Venus symbol design.

title: NOW We've been too nice too long.....
subject: feminism, women's rights, activism
description: A button featuring the logo of the National Organization for Women (NOW) and the slogan 'We've been too nice too long.....', reflecting the assertive stance of second-wave feminism.

title: NOW NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN
subject: Women's Rights, Feminism, Equality
description: A circular black and white button featuring the stylized acronym 'NOW' and the text 'NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN' below it.

title: NOW NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN
subject: Feminism, Women's Rights, Advocacy
description: A black and white circular button featuring the stylized logo of the National Organization for Women (NOW) with the full name of the organization written below.

title: REFUSE TO BE ABUSED
subject: Abuse, Domestic Violence, Activism
description: A purple pin with off-white block lettering that reads 'REFUSE TO BE ABUSED'.

title: LA VOIX DES FEMMES VOICE OF WOMEN, CANADA
subject: Women, Peace, Activism
description: A blue and white circular button for the Canadian Voice of Women for Peace. It features the organization's name in French and English with a central graphic of a dove, a female symbol, and an olive branch labeled 'peace'.

title: STOP SEXUAL HARASSMENT
subject: sexual harassment, social justice, women's rights
description: A magenta button with the phrase 'STOP SEXUAL HARASSMENT' printed in a hand-drawn style font. 'STOP' is in yellow, while the rest is in white, accompanied by a yellow underline.

title: MARCH 31 INTERNATIONAL DAY OF ACTION SAFE CONTRACEPTION AND ABORTION NO FORCED STERILIZATION
subject: Reproductive Rights, Abortion, Contraception
description: A green circular button with black text advocating for reproductive rights, specifically safe contraception and abortion, and opposing forced sterilization, associated with the International Day of Action on March 31, 1979, organized by the International Contraception, Abortion and Sterilisation Campaign (ICASC).

title: FREEDOM OF CHOICE NOW AND FOREVER
subject: Reproductive Rights, Feminism, Women's Rights
description: A pink and light blue button featuring the slogan 'Freedom of Choice Now and Forever'. The word 'NOW' is stylized in the center, representing the National Organization for Women. The button was likely produced in the late 1980s or early 1990s during a period of increased activism for reproductive rights.

title: VOTE Pro-Choice 90 California Abortion Rights Action League
subject: abortion rights, pro-choice, voting
description: A white button with blue and red text promoting pro-choice candidates in the 1990 elections on behalf of the California Abortion Rights Action League.

title: National People's Campaign 212-633-6646 PRO CHOICE WOMEN FIGHT BACK!
subject: Reproductive Rights, Women's Rights, Activism
description: A purple circular button with white text. It features the contact information for the National People's Campaign and the slogan 'WOMEN FIGHT BACK!' with 'PRO CHOICE' replacing the 'O' in 'WOMEN'. The National People's Campaign was an activist organization based in New York City.

title: KEEP YOUR LAWS OFF MY BODY
subject: abortion, reproductive rights, feminism
description: A yellow circular button with green sans-serif text reading 'KEEP YOUR LAWS OFF MY BODY'. This slogan has been a key rally cry for reproductive rights and bodily autonomy since the late 1960s.

title: BC COALITION CLINICS! FOR ABORTION CLINICS
subject: Abortion, Reproductive rights, Canada
description: A yellow pin-back button advocating for the establishment of abortion clinics in British Columbia, produced by the BC Coalition for Abortion Clinics, which was active during the 1980s campaign for reproductive healthcare access.

title: Speak Out FOR CHOICE ON ABORTION
subject: abortion rights, pro-choice, feminism
description: A circular black button with teal text that reads 'Speak Out FOR CHOICE ON ABORTION'. The letter 'C' in 'CHOICE' is stylized to include a female symbol.

title: Students for CHOICE
subject: abortion, feminism, students
description: A red pin-back button with the slogan "Students for CHOICE" in white, featuring a female symbol as part of the letter C.

title: ABORTION - A WOMAN'S RIGHT CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, feminism, reproductive health
description: A black circular button featuring white text. Around the top curve, it says 'ABORTION - A WOMAN'S RIGHT'. Below that, 'CHOICE' is written in a larger, bold font, with a female symbol integrated into the letter 'C'.

title: ABORTION IS A WOMAN'S CHOICE
subject: abortion, women's rights, pro-choice
description: Circular button with a grid pattern and the slogan 'ABORTION IS A WOMAN'S CHOICE' in black block letters. A female symbol is incorporated into the letter 'C' of 'CHOICE'.

title: LEGALIZE FREE-STANDING ABORTION CLINICS CHOICE
subject: Abortion Rights, Pro-Choice, Reproductive Freedom
description: A blue and white button advocating for the legalization of free-standing abortion clinics with the word CHOICE featured prominently.

title: Canadian Abortion Rights Action League CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, pro-choice, Canada
description: A circular blue button featuring the name of the Canadian Abortion Rights Action League (CARAL) curved around the top. The word "CHOICE" is displayed in large, bold white text in the center, with the letter "C" modified to include a female (Venus) symbol. CARAL was a prominent pro-choice advocacy group in Canada active under this name between 1980 and 2005.

title: CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, feminism, reproductive freedom
description: A purple circular button with the word 'CHOICE' in white. The letter 'C' is integrated into a Venus symbol (the female symbol), which is a common icon representing support for reproductive rights and abortion access.

title: NO NEW ABORTION LAW CHOICE
subject: Abortion, Reproductive Rights, Pro-Choice
description: A blue and white pinback button supporting abortion rights. The text "NO NEW ABORTION LAW" is at the top, and "CHOICE" is in the center, with the letter "C" combined with a female symbol.

title: ABORTION IS A MATTER OF CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, women's rights, pro-choice
description: A blue circular button with white text that reads "ABORTION IS A MATTER OF CHOICE". The letter "C" in "CHOICE" is integrated with a female symbol.

title: NO NEW ABORTION LAW CHOICE CHOICE CHOICE
subject: Abortion; Reproductive Rights; Ireland
description: A purple circular button with white text that reads 'NO NEW ABORTION LAW' in the center. The word 'CHOICE' is repeated three times along the bottom edge, with each instance preceded by a Venus (female) symbol. This button is associated with the pro-choice movement in Ireland during the early 1990s, specifically campaigning against restrictive legislation following the 1992 X Case.

title: FULL ACCESS TO FREE ABORTION CHOICE
subject: Abortion, Reproductive rights, Feminism
description: A pink circular button featuring white text that reads 'FULL ACCESS TO FREE ABORTION'. At the bottom, the word 'CHOICE' is printed, where the 'C' is stylized as a female symbol.

title: PRO CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, reproductive rights, activism
description: A circular button with a black background featuring a '4 of clubs' theme to pun the phrase 'For Choice'. It has the word 'PRO' at the top, 'CHOICE' at the bottom, and the number '4' on either side of a central yellow circle containing a black club symbol.

title: Pro-Child Pro-Family PRO-CHOICE California Abortion Rights Action League
subject: abortion, pro-choice, reproductive rights
description: Circular blue button with white text advocating for abortion rights, featuring the slogan "Pro-Child Pro-Family PRO-CHOICE" and the name of the organization California Abortion Rights Action League.

title: A woman's right to control her life REPEAL ALL ABORTION LAWS
subject: Abortion, Women's Rights, Feminism
description: A pro-choice button from the late 1960s or early 1970s featuring the slogan 'A woman's right to control her life REPEAL ALL ABORTION LAWS' in yellow and orange text on a black background.

title: Part of the PRO-CHOICE Majority!
subject: Pro-choice movement, abortion rights, reproductive health
description: A circular pin-back button with the slogan "Part of the PRO-CHOICE Majority!" in black text against a light green background with a blue horizontal brushstroke behind the word "CHOICE". This slogan was heavily used by NARAL (National Abortion Rights Action League) in the late 1980s.

title: ABORT OPERATION RESCUE
subject: Abortion; Operation Rescue; Pro-Choice
description: A white button with the text "ABORT OPERATION RESCUE". "ABORT" is printed in a red stencil font at an angle across "OPERATION RESCUE" which is printed in bold black block letters. The button is a pro-choice protest against the anti-abortion organization Operation Rescue.

title: IF MEN GOT PREGNANT ABORTION WOULD BE A SACRAMENT
subject: Abortion, Feminism, Women's rights
description: A circular button with a yellow, pink, and white background featuring the feminist slogan "IF MEN GOT PREGNANT ABORTION WOULD BE A SACRAMENT" in black hand-lettered style font. A small pink flower icon is located next to the word SACRAMENT.

title: PARENT-HOOD by CHOICE
subject: Reproductive Rights, Family Planning, Planned Parenthood
description: A cream-colored circular button with the words "PARENT-HOOD by CHOICE" printed in a purple serif font.

title: Keep Abortion Legal - and Safe!
subject: abortion, reproductive rights, healthcare
description: A blue and white circular button with the text 'Keep Abortion Legal - and Safe!' advocating for abortion access and reproductive health.

title: CHOICE
subject: Abortion, Reproductive Rights, Pro-Choice
description: A political button advocating for abortion rights, featuring the word 'CHOICE' in purple text on a white background, framed by a wide red border.

title: KEEP ABORTION SAFE AND LEGAL
subject: Abortion rights, Reproductive health, Pro-choice
description: A teal circular pinback button with the slogan 'KEEP ABORTION SAFE AND LEGAL' printed in white serif capital letters. This button is representative of the pro-choice advocacy movement in the United States, which sought to protect the legal status of abortion following the 1973 Roe v. Wade Supreme Court decision.

title: abortion rights for ALL women
subject: Abortion Rights, Sterilization Abuse, Women's Rights
description: A blue pin-back button with pink and white text that reads 'abortion rights for ALL women' in the center, and 'A WOMEN'S LIFE IS A HUMAN LIFE' and 'END STERILIZATION ABUSE' around the perimeter. This button was likely produced by the Committee for Abortion Rights and Against Sterilization Abuse (CARASA), which was active in the late 1970s and 1980s.

title: Roe vs Wade 10 years of choice 1973-1983
subject: Reproductive Rights, Roe v. Wade, Pro-Choice
description: A white and blue button commemorating the 10th anniversary of the Roe v. Wade Supreme Court decision. The design incorporates a candle and a female symbol to represent the number 10.

title: Keep it Funded Keep it Fair
subject: Taxation, Referendum, Public Funding
description: Campaign button supporting Oregon's Ballot Measure 30 in 2004, which proposed a temporary income tax increase to maintain funding for schools and other services.

title: Council Workers Against Corrie No Return to Backstreet Abortions
subject: abortion rights, Corrie Bill, union activism
description: Protest button from the United Kingdom opposing the 1979 Corrie Bill, which sought to restrict the 1967 Abortion Act.

title: FREE ABORTION ON DEMAND
subject: Abortion, Feminism, Reproductive Rights
description: A button with red text that reads 'FREE ABORTION ON DEMAND' surrounding an illustration of a woman breaking chains labeled 'unwanted pregnancies' and 'backstreet abortionists'.

title: MOTHERHOOD BY CHOICE NOT CHANCE
subject: reproductive rights, feminism, family planning
description: A circular purple button featuring the white text 'MOTHERHOOD BY CHOICE NOT CHANCE'. This slogan was widely used by reproductive rights activists and organizations like Planned Parenthood in the late 20th century to advocate for legal access to contraception and abortion.

title: National MARCH for Women's Lives March 9, 1986 Washington, D C March 16, 1986 Los Angeles Keep Abortion & Birth Control SAFE AND LEGAL
subject: Abortion, Birth Control, Women's Rights
description: A circular button promoting the National March for Women's Lives in 1986. It features purple and gold text on a white background, listing march dates for Washington, D.C. and Los Angeles. The bottom portion shows a purple silhouette of a crowd and the slogan 'Keep Abortion & Birth Control SAFE AND LEGAL'.

title: Co-conspirators for CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, reproductive freedom, feminism
description: A blue oval button featuring the phrase 'Co-conspirators for CHOICE' in white lettering. The 'C' in the word 'CHOICE' is stylized with a female (Venus) symbol.

title: NOW Emergency Action for Women's Lives IT'S THE SUPREME COURT SISTER! Washington, D.C. April 22, 2001
subject: Women's Rights, Supreme Court, Activism
description: A square button for the National Organization for Women's 'Emergency Action for Women's Lives' march in Washington, D.C. on April 22, 2001. The message 'IT'S THE SUPREME COURT SISTER!' focuses on the impact of judicial appointments on women's rights.

title: Voters For Choice
subject: abortion, reproductive rights, PAC
description: A rectangular pin-back button with the text 'VOTERS FOR CHOICE' and an abstract graphic of three overlapping faces in black, white, and tan.

title: PRO CHILD PRO FAMILY PRO CHOICE
subject: Abortion, Family Planning, Reproductive Rights
description: A rectangular red, white, and blue button with the slogan 'PRO CHILD PRO FAMILY PRO CHOICE'. This phrasing was popularized by pro-choice organizations like NARAL in the late 1980s to frame reproductive rights as compatible with family values.

title: U.S. OUT OF EL SALVADOR
subject: El Salvador, Foreign policy, Anti-intervention
description: A rectangular yellow button with red text that reads 'U.S. OUT OF EL SALVADOR' advocating against United States military and political intervention in El Salvador during its civil war.

title: CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, pro-choice, women's rights
description: A rectangular yellow button with the word 'CHOICE' printed in maroon outlined serif capital letters, representing the pro-choice movement for reproductive rights.

title: I HAVE A CHOICE AND I WANT TO KEEP IT
subject: Pro-choice, Abortion, Reproductive Rights
description: A square blue and black button with text advocating for reproductive choice and abortion rights.

title: Marching For Women's Lives! Full Access To Free Abortion
subject: Abortion, Reproductive rights, Women's rights
description: A rectangular pin-back button with a pink and white polka dot background and a white jagged starburst. The text 'Marching For Women's Lives!' and 'Full Access To Free Abortion' is written in a green cursive and sans-serif font. This button is associated with pro-choice demonstrations like the 1989 and 1992 March for Women's Lives in Washington, D.C.

title: I'M PRO CHOICE
subject: Abortion, Women's Rights, Reproductive Rights
description: A black circular button with salmon-colored text that reads "I'M PRO CHOICE", expressing support for the right to choose abortion.

title: USE YOUR VOICE. VOTE FOR CHOICE. CARAL
subject: Abortion Rights, Reproductive Rights, Pro-Choice
description: A blue advocacy button featuring the white text 'USE YOUR VOICE. VOTE FOR CHOICE.' with 'CARAL' in smaller print at the bottom right. CARAL stands for the California Abortion Rights Action League, an organization that promotes reproductive freedom.

title: NEVER AGAIN! PLUS JAMAIS!
subject: abortion rights, pro-choice, reproductive rights
description: A pro-choice button featuring a wire coat hanger with a red slash through it, symbolizing opposition to a return to illegal and unsafe abortions. The slogans 'Never Again!' and 'Plus Jamais!' are written in English and French, likely indicating its use in Canadian activism.

title: [No Coat Hanger Symbol]
subject: abortion, pro-choice, reproductive rights
description: A button featuring a black wire coat hanger with a red diagonal slash through it. This iconic symbol of the pro-choice movement represents the dangers of illegal, 'back-alley' abortions and advocates for safe and legal access to reproductive healthcare.

title: Illegal Abortion Never Again!
subject: Abortion Rights, Pro-Choice, Reproductive Rights
description: A teal button featuring a black outline of a wire coat hanger, a symbol of unsafe illegal abortions. The text reads 'ILLEGAL ABORTION' and 'Never Again!' with 'NATIONAL ABORTION RIGHTS ACTION LEAGUE' (NARAL) at the bottom.

title: A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO WORK A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO CHOOSE
subject: feminism, reproductive rights, labor rights
description: A circular yellow button with blue and red text. The top text in blue reads "A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO WORK" and the bottom text in red reads "A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO CHOOSE". The center features a blue circle with a profile illustration of a woman shouting.

title: DEFEND ABORTION RIGHTS!
subject: Abortion, Reproductive Rights, Women's Rights
description: A circular yellow button featuring the slogan "DEFEND ABORTION RIGHTS!" in bold red, sans-serif capital letters. This type of button was commonly used in pro-choice demonstrations and advocacy efforts throughout the late 20th century, particularly around major Supreme Court cases like Webster v. Reproductive Health Services (1989).

title: I'M PRO-CHOICE AND I VOTE
subject: Reproductive Rights, Abortion, Voting
description: A circular pinback button divided horizontally into white and green sections. The top white section features green text reading "I'M PRO-CHOICE" and the bottom green section features white text reading "AND I VOTE". Buttons like this were popularized in the late 1980s by organizations like NARAL to emphasize the political power of pro-choice supporters.

title: ABORTION IS A PERSONAL DECISION
subject: Abortion, Pro-Choice, Women's Rights
description: A circular button with the pro-choice slogan 'ABORTION IS A PERSONAL DECISION' printed in blue sans-serif lettering on a cream-colored background.

title: REPRODUCTIVE FREEDOM FOR ALL WOMEN
subject: Reproductive Rights, Feminism, Abortion
description: A circular white button with purple text and graphics. The words 'REPRODUCTIVE FREEDOM FOR ALL WOMEN' wrap around the outer edge. In the center, there is a group of diverse women's faces. Within the crowd, a sign is held up that displays a coat hanger with a diagonal strike-through line, which is a common symbol for the pro-choice movement and the demand for safe, legal abortion access.

title: U.S. OUT OF MY UTERUS
subject: Reproductive Rights, Feminism, Abortion
description: A circular button featuring the text "U.S. OUT OF MY UTERUS" in red and teal. It is a political statement advocating for reproductive freedom and opposing government interference in bodily autonomy.

title: NO Abortion Law
subject: Abortion rights, Reproductive rights, Repeal movements
description: A white button with blue text reading "NO Abortion Law". The word "NO" is in a large, bold, underlined sans-serif font, while "Abortion Law" is in a smaller serif font. This type of messaging was common among pre-Roe v. Wade activists who favored the total repeal of abortion laws over moderate reform measures.

title: PAIGC FRELIMO MPLA
subject: African liberation, Portuguese colonialism, Solidarity movements
description: A yellow button featuring the acronyms of three African liberation movements: PAIGC (African Party for the Independence of Guinea and Cape Verde), FRELIMO (Liberation Front of Mozambique), and MPLA (People's Movement for the Liberation of Angola). The center contains an outline of the African continent overlaid with a rifle and three stars.

title: Organização da Mulher de Angola O.M.A.
subject: Women, Independence, Angola
description: A red button featuring a silhouette of a woman carrying a rifle, representing the Organização da Mulher de Angola (OMA), the women's wing of the MPLA.

title: Angola for Angolans MPLA
subject: Angola, independence, MPLA
description: A green circular button featuring an outline of Africa with Angola highlighted. The text 'Angola for Angolans' arches around the top and bottom, with 'MPLA' in the center.

title: U.S. HANDS OFF ANGOLA! YOUNG SOCIALIST ALLIANCE
subject: Socialism, Angola, Anti-interventionism
description: A green and red circular button featuring a black map of Africa. The red text 'U.S. HANDS OFF ANGOLA!' is written across the map, and 'YOUNG SOCIALIST ALLIANCE' is written along the bottom border.

title: MPLA for ANGOLA
subject: Angola, Liberation movements, Solidarity
description: Button supporting the People's Movement for the Liberation of Angola (MPLA). The MPLA was a major political party and liberation movement that fought for Angolan independence from Portugal.

title: ETHIOPIA CRISIS FUND world university service of canada
subject: Famine relief, Ethiopia, Humanitarian aid
description: A circular button from the mid-1980s promoting the Ethiopia Crisis Fund, an initiative by the World University Service of Canada (WUSC) to provide relief during the 1983-1985 Ethiopian famine. The center features the WUSC logo surrounded by text.

title: DIVEST BOSTON UNIVERSITY
subject: Divestment, Apartheid, South Africa
description: A red, black, and white button advocating for Boston University to divest its endowment from companies doing business in South Africa during the apartheid era.

title: CHAD GENOCIDE THE WORLD IS WATCHING

description: This button raises awareness of the genocide in Chad. It features red, jagged text stating 'CHAD GENOCIDE THE WORLD IS WATCHING' superimposed over a map of Africa and two crossed poles colored blue, yellow, and red, representing the flag of Chad.

title: Stop Genocides Now
subject: Genocide, Human Rights, Activism
description: A circular black button with white text that reads "Stop Genocides Now" centered in the middle.

title: I Haven't Forgotten Africa Oxfam

description: A white pinback button featuring a red map of Africa. The text 'I Haven't Forgotten' wraps around the top, with 'Africa' across the map and 'Oxfam' at the bottom. This button was part of a mid-1980s campaign to maintain public support for famine relief efforts.

title: NO TORY SELL-OUT IN ZIMBABWE
subject: Zimbabwe, UK foreign policy, Decolonization
description: Yellow button with red text reading 'NO TORY SELL-OUT IN ZIMBABWE'. It refers to British foreign policy during the transition of Rhodesia to Zimbabwe in the late 1970s, specifically targeting the Conservative Party's handling of the independence negotiations.

title: FREE NAMIBIA BRITAIN'S NUCLEAR COLONY CANUC
subject: Namibia, Uranium, Independence
description: Yellow button featuring an illustration of a miner's face, produced by the Campaign Against the Namibian Uranium Contracts (CANUC). It calls for Namibian independence and criticizes British involvement in uranium mining during the South African occupation of Namibia.

title: END RACIST OCCUPATION OF NAMIBIA
subject: Namibian Independence, Anti-Apartheid, SWAPO
description: A white button with the text 'END RACIST OCCUPATION OF NAMIBIA'. The word 'END' is in red while the rest of the text is in black. The button features a black and white illustration of a man, likely Sam Nujoma, with a raised fist in a power salute.

title: JOIN THE CAMPAIGN NOW! OPERATION BUILD LIBERIA
subject: Liberia, National Development, Fundraising
description: A circular red button with a white outline map of Liberia. Text in the center says "OPERATION BUILD LIBERIA" and text around the upper edge says "JOIN THE CAMPAIGN NOW!". This button likely relates to the 'Rally Time' or 'Operation Build Liberia' campaign launched by President William R. Tolbert, Jr. to fund national development projects.

title: LIFELINE for BIAFRA Oct. 25 HELP KEEP THE CANDLE BURNING
subject: Biafra, Humanitarian Aid, Nigerian Civil War
description: A campaign button for the Lifeline for Biafra organization, likely referencing a fundraiser or day of awareness on October 25, 1969, during the Nigerian Civil War. It depicts a starving child and a candle.

title: SAVE STARVING BIAFRA STUDENT MOBILIZATION FOR BIAFRA

description: This black and white pinback button advocates for humanitarian aid during the Nigerian Civil War (1967-1970). It features the text "SAVE STARVING BIAFRA" in the center and "STUDENT MOBILIZATION FOR BIAFRA" along the bottom edge. The group was part of a global movement to bring attention to the famine in the secessionist Republic of Biafra.

title: KEEP BIAFRA ALIVE
subject: Biafra, Nigeria, Civil War
description: A white button with black text reading 'KEEP BIAFRA ALIVE'. This button was likely used to raise awareness and support for the Republic of Biafra during the Nigerian Civil War (1967-1970).

title: RECOGNIZE BIAFRA
subject: Biafra, Nigerian Civil War, Secession
description: A pin-back button featuring the text 'RECOGNIZE BIAFRA' over a graphic of a rising sun, the symbol of the Republic of Biafra. The background colors are red, black, and green, which are the colors of the Biafran flag. This button was likely produced during the Nigerian Civil War (1967-1970) to support international recognition of Biafra's independence.

title: HELP BIAFRA NOW BIAFRA RELIEF SERVICES FOUNDATION
subject: Biafra, Humanitarian Aid, Nigerian Civil War
description: This button was produced by the Biafra Relief Services Foundation to raise awareness and support for humanitarian aid during the Nigerian Civil War (1967-1970), a conflict marked by a severe famine in the secessionist Republic of Biafra.

title: SOLIDARITY WITH SWAPO WOMEN
subject: Namibia, Women's Rights, Liberation

title: Free East Timor August 25 Withdraw Recognition of Indonesian Annexation National Day of Solidarity
subject: East Timor Independence, Human Rights, Solidarity
description: A red pin-back button advocating for the independence of East Timor and protesting the Indonesian annexation. It features the text 'Free EAST TIMOR' in large block letters in the center, with 'August 25' below it. Around the edge, it reads 'Withdraw Recognition of Indonesian Annexation' and 'National Day of Solidarity'.

title: INDEPENDENCE ● FREEDOM FRETILIN FOR EAST TIMOR
party: FRETILIN (Revolutionary Front for an Independent East Timor)

title: Idaho wins with Church
subject: Frank Church, Idaho, Political campaign
description: A campaign button for Frank Church's re-election campaign to the United States Senate from Idaho. The button features a stylized red 'I' and the text 'Idaho wins with Church' in blue lettering on a white background.

title: THIS IOWAN WANTS REAGAN - BUSH '80
subject: 1980 Presidential Election, Reagan/Bush Campaign, Iowa
description: A 1980 campaign button for the Republican presidential ticket of Ronald Reagan and George H.W. Bush. The button is specifically designed for Iowa voters, featuring portraits of both candidates and incorporating the Iowa state motto, 'Our liberties we prize and our rights we will maintain,' along with the campaign slogan 'Together... A New Beginning.'

title: DEMOCRAT MAGRANN U.S. CONGRESS
subject: Election, Democrat, Congress
description: Blue and white campaign button for Thomas J. Magrann for the United States House of Representatives from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

title: John V. Lindsay '72
subject: Presidential Campaign, 1972 Election, John V. Lindsay
description: A campaign button for John V. Lindsay's 1972 presidential bid, featuring red and white text on a blue background.

title: SCIENTISTS for McGovern
subject: George McGovern, presidential campaign, scientists
description: A blue and white metal campaign tab from George McGovern's 1972 presidential campaign. The bottom rectangular section has a blue background with white text reading 'SCIENTISTS for McGovern'. The top circular section is white and features a blue Lithographers and Photoengravers International Union (LPIU) bug.

title: Scott Harshbarger '90
subject: Political Campaign, Massachusetts, Attorney General
description: A green and white political button for Scott Harshbarger's 1990 campaign for Massachusetts Attorney General.

title: PAUL WHITE
subject: Elections, Ohio, Congress
description: A red and white campaign button for Paul White, a Democratic candidate for the US House of Representatives from Ohio's 11th district in 1974.

title: Gus Hall Jarvis Tyner VOTE COMMUNIST IN 1972
subject: Communist Party, Presidential Election, 1972
description: A campaign button for the Communist Party USA candidates Gus Hall (for President) and Jarvis Tyner (for Vice President) in the 1972 US presidential election.

title: Jim Shannon U.S. SENATE
subject: elections, politics, senate
description: A campaign button for Jim Shannon's 1984 bid for the U.S. Senate in Massachusetts.

title: COLLINS CONGRESS
subject: Republican, Congress, Election
description: A white political campaign button for Mac Collins' run for the U.S. House of Representatives. It features an American flag graphic next to the text 'COLLINS CONGRESS' in blue ink.

title: RE-ELECT FANTINI School Committee Vote #1
subject: Local elections, Education, Ranked-choice voting
description: Campaign button supporting the re-election of Alfred B. Fantini to the Cambridge School Committee. The design includes 'Vote #1', which is characteristic of the ranked-choice voting system used in Cambridge, Massachusetts.

title: Joe Kennedy CONGRESS
subject: Kennedy family, US House of Representatives, Democratic Party
description: A campaign button for Joseph P. Kennedy II's run for the U.S. House of Representatives in Massachusetts, featuring blue and red text on a white background.

title: A WINNING TEAM AMERICA NEEDS THEM
subject: Jimmy Carter, Walter Mondale, 1980 Election
description: A red political campaign button for the Carter-Mondale presidential ticket, featuring black and white portraits of the candidates and the slogans 'A Winning Team' and 'America Needs Them'.

title: DUKAKIS BENTSEN
subject: Presidential Campaign, Democratic Party, 1988 Election
description: A white button featuring the names of Democratic presidential and vice-presidential candidates Michael Dukakis and Lloyd Bentsen in red text, including an Allied Printing Trades Council union bug from Toledo, Ohio.

title: Believe Muskie.
subject: Presidential Campaign, Edmund Muskie, Democratic Party
description: A white political button featuring the slogan "Believe Muskie." in bold red text, used during Edmund Muskie's 1972 campaign for the Democratic presidential nomination.

title: HGEA AFSCME Waihee GOV. (D) Cayetano LT. GOV. (D)
person: John D. Waiheʹe III, Benjamin Cayetano
subject: Hawaii, Unions, Governor
description: A campaign button for the John Waihee and Ben Cayetano ticket for Governor and Lieutenant Governor of Hawaii, endorsed by HGEA and AFSCME. The button features the flag of Hawaii in the background and the names of the candidates.

title: KING DEMOCRAT FOR GOVERNOR
subject: Bruce King, New Mexico, Governor
description: A white campaign button for Bruce King's gubernatorial race in New Mexico. It features the name 'KING' in large blue sans-serif letters within a thin red rectangular border, with a red dot over the letter 'I'. Below the name is the phrase 'DEMOCRAT FOR GOVERNOR' in red capital letters.

title: FOR THE LEADERSHIP WE NEED PRESIDENT
subject: John F. Kennedy, 1960 Campaign, Presidential Leadership
description: A campaign button from John F. Kennedy's 1960 presidential run featuring a black and white portrait of Kennedy in the center with blue text at the top and white text on a red banner at the bottom.

title: McG
subject: McGovern, 1972, Election
description: A gold-colored die-cut metal pin with the letters 'McG', representing George McGovern's 1972 presidential campaign.

title: Askew FOR PRESIDENT
subject: Reubin Askew, Presidential Campaign, 1984 Election
description: A white button with 'Askew' in large orange font and 'FOR PRESIDENT' in smaller black font above it. A small star is inside the letter 'A'. This button was likely used during Reubin Askew's 1984 presidential campaign.

title: LANGONE
subject: Politics; Election; Campaign
description: A white button with the name 'LANGONE' in red text, featuring a 1986 date next to a union bug, likely for Frederick C. Langone's campaign for the Massachusetts Governor's Council.

title: Lick Dick in '72
subject: Richard Nixon, 1972 Election, Slogan
description: A white campaign button featuring the text "Lick Dick in '72" in blue script, surrounded by a red and blue decorative border with white stars. The button refers to Richard Nixon's 1972 re-election campaign and utilizes a play on his name that was used both as a genuine (if provocative) slogan and as satire.

title: CONGRESS RIZZO 5TH DISTRICT
subject: political campaign, congress, Frank Rizzo
description: A political campaign button produced during Frank Rizzo's brief consideration of a run for Pennsylvania's 5th congressional district in 1986. Rizzo, a former Democratic mayor of Philadelphia, had recently switched to the Republican Party at the time.

title: I LIKE MARY for SECRETARY
subject: Campaign, Election, Secretary
description: A round white button with teal text that reads 'I LIKE MARY for SECRETARY'. The slogan is likely a play on the famous 'I Like Ike' campaign slogan used for Dwight D. Eisenhower.

title: RE-ELECT SERRA REPRESENTATIVE
subject: election, campaign, Connecticut
description: A green campaign button with white text reading 'RE-ELECT SERRA REPRESENTATIVE', used for Joseph C. Serra's long-running tenure in the Connecticut House of Representatives.

title: John Sears Per Sindaco
subject: Mayoral election, Italian-American voters, Boston
description: A political campaign button for John W. Sears' candidacy for Mayor of Boston. The text 'Per Sindaco' is Italian for 'For Mayor', intended to appeal to the city's significant Italian-American population.

title: Confidence - Peace - Prosperity FORD-DOLE in '76
subject: 1976 Presidential Election
description: A round political campaign button supporting Gerald Ford and Bob Dole in the 1976 election. The button features black and white photographic portraits of the candidates in the center, with a red top border displaying the words "Confidence - Peace - Prosperity" and a blue bottom border reading "FORD-DOLE in '76".

title: 4 LBJ
subject: Presidential Campaign, 1964, Lyndon B. Johnson
description: A circular red, white, and blue campaign button supporting Lyndon B. Johnson in the 1964 presidential election. The top half is red with a large white digit 4, and the bottom half is blue with the white initials LBJ.

title: LOWENSTEIN FOR CONGRESS
subject: Political campaign, Allard K. Lowenstein, U.S. House of Representatives
description: An orange, white, and blue campaign button supporting Allard K. Lowenstein for the United States House of Representatives.

title: Abby Browne Boston School Committee Beacon Hill Harbor Towers Mission Hill Back Bay Fenway
subject: Local Politics, Education, School Board
description: White button with brown text and an illustration of a yellow pencil for Abby Browne's campaign for the Boston School Committee. The button lists several Boston neighborhoods that Browne represented in District 8: Beacon Hill, Harbor Towers, Mission Hill, Back Bay, and Fenway.

title: james michael CONNOLLY
subject: campaign, local, Massachusetts
description: A yellow political campaign button with green text for James Michael Connolly, likely for his run for the Massachusetts House of Representatives where he served from 1969 to 1978.

title: I'm for RUFUS
subject: Politics, Campaign, Gubernatorial
description: A campaign button for Rufus Edmisten, likely used during his 1984 gubernatorial campaign in North Carolina. The button is split vertically with red on the left and blue on the right, featuring white text.

title: RE-ELECT CONTE TO CONGRESS
subject: Re-election, US Congress, Massachusetts
description: A campaign button for Silvio O. Conte, a Republican who represented Massachusetts's 1st district in the U.S. House of Representatives from 1959 until his death in 1991.

title: FINNEGAN OR HIM AGAIN
subject: elections, state politics, candidates
description: A campaign button for Richard J. Finnegan during his 1988 run for the Massachusetts State Senate. The slogan 'Finnegan or Him Again' was used to challenge the incumbent opponent.

title: JOHN SEARS for GOVERNOR
subject: John Sears, Governor, Election
description: A blue and white campaign button supporting John Sears for the office of Governor of Massachusetts in 1982.
